title: Legislators
exposure_rationale: Legislators primarily engage in planning, organizing, and evaluating within government and business contexts, which involves significant interpersonal and judgment-based tasks. While AI can assist with drafting documents, analyzing data, and managing schedules, the core responsibilities of decision-making and public engagement require human judgment and political acumen. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and leadership roles of legislators.

title: Senior government managers and officials
education_req: A university degree or college diploma is usually required.
A graduate degree in a related field may be required.
Several years of managerial experience in the public or private sector are required.
exposure_rationale: Senior government managers and officials engage in planning, organizing, directing, and evaluating, which involves significant coordination and decision-making. While AI can assist with data analysis and administrative tasks, the role requires human judgment, leadership, and strategic decision-making that are less susceptible to full automation. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance productivity, but Tier 3 multi-agent systems are unlikely to replace the core leadership and evaluative functions of this role.

title: Senior managers - financial, communications and other business services
education_req: A university degree in business administration, commerce, computer science or other discipline related to the service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in financial, communications or other business services are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or service is possible through specific university training in that area or through previous experience.
exposure_rationale: Senior managers in financial, communications, and business services are highly exposed to AI due to their focus on planning, organizing, directing, and evaluating business operations. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can significantly enhance productivity by automating routine decision-making, scheduling, and data analysis tasks. However, human judgment, leadership, and strategic decision-making remain central, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Senior managers - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in business administration, hospital administration, finance or other discipline related to the service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in a related institution or organization are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or service is possible through specific university or college training in that area or through previous experience.
exposure_rationale: Senior managers in health, education, social and community services are involved in strategic planning, decision-making, and overseeing operations, which are tasks less susceptible to full automation. However, AI tools can assist significantly in data analysis, report generation, and scheduling, enhancing productivity. Tier 2 and Tier 3 systems can automate routine coordination and communication tasks, but human judgment and leadership remain crucial, keeping the exposure at a high but not maximum level.

title: Senior managers - trade, broadcasting and other services
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in business administration, finance or other discipline related to the service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in trade, broadcasting or other service are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or service is possible through specific university or college training in that area or through previous experience.
exposure_rationale: Senior managers in trade, broadcasting, and other services are primarily involved in strategic planning, decision-making, and high-level coordination. While AI can assist with data analysis, reporting, and some routine decision-making, the role still requires significant human judgment, leadership, and interpersonal skills. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools can enhance productivity but are unlikely to fully automate the strategic and evaluative aspects of the role.

title: Senior managers - construction, transportation, production and utilities
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in engineering, business administration, commerce or other discipline related to the company's product is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in goods production, utilities, transportation or construction are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or product is possible through specific university or college training in that area or through previous experience.
exposure_rationale: Senior managers in construction, transportation, production, and utilities engage in planning, organizing, and evaluating, which are tasks that can be supported by AI tools for data analysis and decision-making. However, the role requires significant human judgment, leadership, and experience-based decision-making that AI cannot fully replicate. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with scheduling and coordination, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the strategic and high-level nature of the work.

title: Financial managers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in business administration, economics, commerce or a related field is required.
A master's degree in business administration (concentration in finance), or another master's level management program may be required.
Several years of experience in accounting, auditing, budgeting, financial planning and analysis or other financial activities are required.
exposure_rationale: Financial managers are exposed to AI through tasks involving data analysis, financial reporting, and strategic planning, where LLMs and LLM+tools can enhance productivity. However, human judgment, regulatory compliance, and decision-making remain central to the role, limiting full automation. Multi-agent systems may automate routine financial coordination tasks, but the strategic and leadership aspects of the role maintain a significant human element.

title: Human resources managers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in a field related to personnel management, such as business administration, industrial relations, commerce or psychology or completion of a professional development program in personnel administration is required.
Several years of experience as a personnel officer or human resource specialist are required.
Some employers may require human resources managers to hold a Certified Human Resources Professional (CHRP) designation.
exposure_rationale: Human resources managers are exposed to AI primarily through Tier 2 and Tier 3 systems that can automate scheduling, communication, and routine decision-making tasks. While AI can handle significant portions of administrative tasks, human judgment, client trust, and regulatory accountability remain central to the role. The occupation involves strategic planning and interpersonal skills that are less susceptible to full automation.

title: Purchasing managers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce or economics is usually required.

Purchasing managers responsible for units purchasing specialized materials or business services may require a related degree or diploma. For example, a bachelor's degree or college diploma in engineering may be required for purchasing managers responsible for purchasing industrial products.
The designation Supply Chain Management Professional (SCMP) or registration in the educational program of the Supply Chain Management Association may be required.
exposure_rationale: Purchasing managers are highly exposed to AI due to their focus on coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with data analysis, supplier communication, and contract management, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine procurement workflows, such as order processing and inventory management. Human judgment and strategic decision-making remain important, but AI is poised to significantly impact efficiency and task automation in this role.

title: Other administrative services managers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in business administration or a related administrative services field is usually required.
Several years of experience at a professional level in business administration, finance or administrative services are usually required.

An Associate of the Institute of the Chartered Secretaries and Administrators (ACIS), Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Secretaries and Administrators (FCIS) or a Professional Administrator (P Adm) designation may be required for some occupations in this group.
exposure_rationale: Other administrative services managers are primarily involved in planning, organizing, directing, and controlling various business functions, which are tasks increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 (LLM+tools) and Tier 3 (multi-agent systems) can significantly enhance productivity by automating routine coordination, scheduling, and communication tasks. However, human judgment and strategic decision-making remain crucial, preventing full automation and keeping exposure from reaching the highest levels.

title: Insurance, real estate and financial brokerage managers
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in business administration, economics or other related field is usually required.
Several years of experience within the appropriate industry are usually required.
Licensure appropriate to the service sold, such as real estate, mortgage, securities or insurance, may be required.
exposure_rationale: Insurance, real estate, and financial brokerage managers are primarily involved in planning, organizing, and evaluating business operations, which involves significant coordination and communication tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with scheduling, report generation, and data analysis, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine decision-making and coordination tasks. However, human judgment, client trust, and regulatory compliance remain critical, limiting full automation potential.

title: Banking, credit and other investment managers
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce, economics or a related field is usually required.
A master's degree in business administration, finance or management science may be required for the management of large commercial loans.
A recognized financial designation may be required (CFA, CFP, CIM or others).
exposure_rationale: Banking, credit and investment managers are highly exposed to AI due to the significant amount of knowledge work, coordination, and communication involved in their roles. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with data analysis, report generation, and customer communication, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine decision-making and workflow management. However, human judgment and regulatory accountability remain crucial, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Advertising, marketing and public relations managers
education_req: Advertising and public relations managers

A university degree or college diploma in communications, public relations, marketing, journalism or in a related field and several years of experience in an advertising, public relations or communications officer position or in a related occupation are required.
Marketing managers
exposure_rationale: Advertising, marketing, and public relations managers are highly exposed to AI due to the digital nature of their work, which involves planning, organizing, and evaluating campaigns. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with data analysis, customer segmentation, and campaign optimization, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine coordination tasks such as scheduling and reporting. Human judgment and creativity remain important, but AI is increasingly capable of handling significant portions of the workflow.

title: Other business services managers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in a field related to the business service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience in a field related to the business service provided are usually required.
Licences, certificates or registration may be required for some business services managers. For example, accounting firm managers usually require accounting certification.
exposure_rationale: Other business services managers are primarily involved in planning, organizing, directing, and evaluating business operations, which are tasks that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist in scheduling, data analysis, and report generation, while Tier 3 AI (multi-agent systems) could automate routine coordination and communication tasks. However, human judgment and strategic decision-making remain central, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Telecommunication carriers managers
education_req: A university degree in science, electrical engineering or a related field is usually required.
Several years of experience in a related technical profession, including supervisory experience, are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Telecommunication carriers managers are involved in planning, organizing, directing, and evaluating operations, which are predominantly knowledge and coordination tasks. AI tools, particularly Tier 2 (LLM+tools), can assist significantly in data analysis, reporting, and communication tasks. However, human judgment and strategic decision-making remain central, limiting full automation potential. Tier 3 multi-agent systems may further streamline routine coordination tasks but are unlikely to replace the strategic aspects of the role entirely.

title: Financial auditors and accountants
education_req: Chartered professional accountants, chartered accountants (CPA, CA) require a university degree and completion of a professional training program approved by a provincial Institute of Chartered Accountants and, depending on the province, either two years or 30 months of on-the-job training and membership in a provincial Institute of Chartered Accountants upon successful completion of the Uniform Evaluation (UFE).

Chartered professional accountants, certified general accountants (CPA, CGA) and chartered professional accountants, certified management accountants (CPA, CMA) require a university degree and completion of an approved training program and several years of on-the-job training and certification with a regulatory body is required in all provinces and territories.

Auditors require education, training and recognition as indicated for chartered professional accountants, chartered accountants (CPA, CA), chartered professional accountants, certified general accountants (CPA, CGA) or chartered professional accountants, certified management accountants (CPA, CMA) and some experience as an accountant.
exposure_rationale: Financial auditors and accountants are highly exposed to AI due to the nature of their work, which involves significant data analysis, report generation, and compliance tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can automate many routine tasks such as data entry, report drafting, and initial compliance checks, while Tier 3 systems could further streamline workflows by automating end-to-end processes like financial audits and tax filings. Human judgment and regulatory accountability remain important, but AI is poised to significantly enhance productivity and reshape task allocation in this field.

title: Financial and investment analysts
education_req: A bachelor's degree in commerce, business administration, accounting, finance or economics and on-the-job training and industry courses and programs are usually required.
A master's degree in business administration (MBA) (concentration in finance) or in finance may be required.
The Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) designation, available through a program conducted by the CFA Institute or an other recognized financial designations (CFP, CIM or others), is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Financial and investment analysts are highly exposed to AI due to their reliance on data analysis, forecasting, and report generation, which can be significantly enhanced by LLMs and LLM+tools. AI can automate data processing, trend analysis, and even generate investment recommendations, reducing the need for human intervention in routine tasks. While human judgment and regulatory compliance remain important, multi-agent systems could further streamline workflows, increasing exposure to AI-driven changes.

title: Financial advisors
education_req: A bachelor's degree in business administration, commerce, economics or a related field is usually required.
The designation Certified Financial Planner, awarded by the Financial Planners Standards Council of Canada, may be required.
To sell regulated financial products and investments, such as annuities, RRSPs and life insurance, a licence is required from the appropriate governing body.
exposure_rationale: Financial advisors are highly exposed to AI due to the predominance of knowledge work, coordination, and communication tasks. LLM+tools can assist with data analysis, report generation, and client communication, while multi-agent systems can automate routine financial planning and scheduling tasks. Human judgment and client trust remain important, but AI is increasingly capable of handling significant portions of the advisory process.

title: Securities agents, investment dealers and brokers
education_req: A bachelor's or master's degree in economics, business or other discipline is usually required.
A recognized financial designation may be required (CFA, CFP, CIM or others).

Securities sales representatives and brokers require completion of industry investment and sales training programs and completion of licensing courses such as the Canadian Securities Course and the Conduct and Practices Handbook Course offered by the Canadian Securities Institute, or acceptable alternatives such as completion of one or more levels of the CFA Program, offered by CFA Institute.
exposure_rationale: Securities agents, investment dealers, and brokers are highly exposed to AI due to the digital nature of their work, which involves analyzing market data, executing trades, and managing client portfolios. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with data analysis, report generation, and client communication, while Tier 3 systems can automate routine trading and portfolio management tasks. Human judgment and client trust remain important, but AI is rapidly advancing in these areas, increasing exposure significantly.

title: Other financial officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in business administration, commerce, economics or a related field is usually required.

Various training programs and courses are offered by financial institutes and organizations, such as the Canadian Securities Institute, Institute of Canadian Bankers, CFA Institute, Investment Funds Institute and Trust Companies Institute of Canada, and may be required by employers.
A recognized financial designation may be required (CFA, CIM or others).
exposure_rationale: Other financial officers engage in tasks such as financial analysis, reporting, and coordination, which are highly susceptible to AI tools. Tier 2 AI systems can automate significant portions of data analysis, report generation, and routine financial tasks, while Tier 3 systems could further streamline workflows involving financial coordination and communication. Human judgment and regulatory compliance remain important, but AI is poised to enhance productivity and automate sub-tasks significantly in this field.

title: Human resources professionals
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in human resources management or a related field, such as business administration, industrial relations, commerce or psychology or completion of a professional development program in human resources administration is required.
Some employers may require human resources professionals to hold a Certified Human Resources Professional (CHRP) designation.
exposure_rationale: Human resources professionals engage in tasks such as recruitment, employee relations, and policy development, which involve significant coordination and communication. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with scheduling interviews, processing applications, and managing employee data, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine HR workflows like initial candidate screening and onboarding processes. However, human judgment and interpersonal skills remain crucial for complex decision-making and sensitive employee interactions, moderating the overall AI exposure.

title: Professional occupations in business management consulting
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce or a related discipline is usually required.
A graduate degree in business administration may be required.
Some establishments may require management consultants to be certified by a provincial management consulting association.
exposure_rationale: Professional occupations in business management consulting involve significant knowledge work, coordination, and communication tasks. AI tools, particularly LLM+tools and multi-agent systems, can enhance productivity by automating data analysis, report generation, and routine client communication. However, human judgment, client trust, and strategic decision-making remain central, preventing full automation. The exposure is high due to the potential for AI to handle substantial portions of administrative and analytical tasks.

title: Professional occupations in advertising, marketing and public relations
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in business marketing, public relations, communications, journalism, museology or a discipline related to a particular subject matter is usually required.
Practitioners in public relations may require an APR (Accredited in Public Relations) designation.
exposure_rationale: Professional occupations in advertising, marketing, and public relations are highly exposed to AI due to the digital nature of tasks such as content creation, data analysis, and campaign management. Tier 2 AI tools can automate scheduling, reporting, and customer interaction tasks, while Tier 3 systems can manage entire marketing workflows, from strategy development to execution and analysis. Human creativity and strategic oversight remain important, but AI is rapidly advancing in these areas, increasing exposure significantly.

title: Supervisors, general office and administrative support workers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of college courses related to area supervised is usually required.
Experience in the occupation supervised is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Supervisors in general office and administrative support roles are highly exposed to AI due to the predominance of coordination, communication, and routine decision-making tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can significantly enhance productivity by automating scheduling, report generation, and correspondence. Tier 3 multi-agent systems could further automate workflows, reducing the need for human oversight in routine administrative processes, though human judgment and leadership remain important for complex decision-making and team management.

title: Supervisors, finance and insurance office workers
education_req: Completion of a college program or courses in bookkeeping, accounting or a related subject is required.
Experience in the clerical occupation supervised is required.
exposure_rationale: Supervisors in finance and insurance office roles are highly exposed to AI due to the predominance of coordination, communication, and administrative tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can automate significant portions of scheduling, report generation, and routine decision-making. While human judgment and regulatory compliance remain important, multi-agent systems could further streamline workflows, increasing exposure relative to base LLMs alone.

title: Supervisors, library, correspondence and related information workers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of college courses related to area supervised may be required.
Experience in the clerical occupation supervised is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Supervisors in library, correspondence, and related information roles are highly exposed to AI due to the nature of their work, which involves significant coordination, communication, and information management tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can automate scheduling, correspondence, and database management, while Tier 3 systems could streamline entire workflows, reducing the need for human oversight in routine tasks. However, human judgment and supervisory roles still play a crucial part, preventing maximum exposure.

title: Supervisors, supply chain, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
education_req: Completion of a post-secondary education program of two to three years in business, supply chain, production or manufacturing management and related is usually required.
Several years of experience in the occupation supervised are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Supervisors in supply chain, tracking, and scheduling coordination are highly exposed to AI due to the nature of their work, which involves significant coordination, scheduling, and communication tasks. Tier 2 AI systems can already automate many of these tasks, such as scheduling and tracking shipments, while Tier 3 systems could further streamline entire workflows, reducing the need for human intervention. However, human judgment and oversight remain important for managing complex logistics and addressing exceptions, which moderates the exposure slightly.

title: Executive assistants
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A bachelor's degree in public administration, political science or a related discipline is usually required.
Experience in a related administrative occupation is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Executive assistants are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks of scheduling, communication, and coordination, which are squarely in the domain of LLM+tools and multi-agent systems. These systems can automate tasks like managing calendars, booking travel, and drafting correspondence, significantly reducing the need for human intervention. While human judgment and interpersonal skills remain relevant, the rapid improvement in AI tools suggests major restructuring is likely within 5 years.

title: Human resources and recruitment officers
education_req: A college diploma in a field related to personnel management such as business administration, industrial relations, commerce or completion of a professional development program in personnel administration is usually required.
Certification as a Certified Human Resources Professional (CHRP) may be required.
Some experience in a clerical or administrative position related to personnel administration may be required.
exposure_rationale: Human resources and recruitment officers are highly exposed to AI, particularly through Tier 2 and Tier 3 systems. Tasks such as screening résumés, scheduling interviews, and managing candidate databases can be significantly automated with LLM+tools and multi-agent systems. While human judgment and interpersonal skills remain important, the core administrative and coordination tasks are increasingly handled by AI, driving a high exposure score.

title: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers
education_req: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers usually require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce or economics.

Procurement and purchasing agents and officers purchasing specialized materials or business services may require a related university degree or college diploma. For example, a bachelor's degree or college diploma in engineering may be required for purchasers of industrial products.
A certificate in purchasing from the Purchasing Management Association of Canada (PMAC) may be required.
exposure_rationale: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers are highly exposed to AI due to the digital nature of their tasks, which include analysing supplier data, negotiating contracts, and managing procurement processes. Tier 2 AI tools can automate data analysis, supplier communication, and contract drafting, while Tier 3 systems can handle end-to-end procurement workflows, significantly reducing the need for human intervention in routine tasks. The occupation's reliance on digital coordination and decision-making makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven transformation.

title: Conference and event planners
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in business, tourism or hospitality administration is usually required.
Several years of experience in hospitality or tourism administration or in public relations are usually required and may substitute for formal education requirements.
Certification relating to special events, meetings or conference management may be required.
exposure_rationale: Conference and event planners are highly exposed to AI due to the significant coordination, scheduling, and communication tasks involved in their roles. Tier 2 AI systems can automate tasks such as booking venues, managing schedules, and handling correspondence, while Tier 3 systems could potentially manage entire event workflows. Human judgment and creativity remain important for unique event design and client interactions, but AI tools are increasingly capable of handling routine planning and administrative tasks.

title: Employment insurance and revenue officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma is usually required.
Several years of related administrative or regulatory experience may be required.
Completion of specialized government training is required.
exposure_rationale: Employment insurance and revenue officers primarily engage in digital tasks such as processing claims, managing records, and ensuring compliance with regulations. Tier 2 AI tools can significantly enhance productivity by automating data entry, report generation, and routine correspondence. Tier 3 multi-agent systems could further automate workflows, including claim processing and communication, reducing the need for human intervention in routine tasks.

title: Court reporters, medical transcriptionists and related occupations
education_req: Court reporters require completion of secondary school.
Completion of a college or other program in court reporting is usually required and the Chartered Shorthand Reporter (CSR) certificate may be required for court reporters.
Medical transcriptionists require completion of secondary school.
exposure_rationale: Court reporters and medical transcriptionists are highly exposed to AI as their core tasks involve routine information processing, transcription, and documentation, which are squarely in the domain of LLMs and multi-agent systems. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI can handle transcription, summarization, and document management with high accuracy and efficiency, significantly reducing the need for human involvement in these tasks. The digital nature and repetitive workflow of these roles make them particularly susceptible to AI-driven automation.

title: Health information management occupations
education_req: Health information management technicians and related workers require completion of a recognized two-year college diploma program in health record technology or health information management.

Health information management technicians and related workers are eligible to write the Canadian Health Information Management Association national certification examination which may be required by employers.
exposure_rationale: Health information management occupations involve significant digital coordination, data analysis, and administrative tasks, which are highly exposed to AI advancements. Tier 2 (LLM+tools) can automate data processing, report generation, and routine administrative tasks, while Tier 3 (multi-agent systems) could further streamline workflows, reducing the need for human intervention in routine information management. The core tasks are predominantly digital, making them susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains.

title: Records management technicians
education_req: Records management technicians usually require completion of a two- to three-year college program in information or records management technology.
Two to three years' experience in a records management environment may be required.
exposure_rationale: Records management technicians primarily handle digital tasks such as organizing, storing, and retrieving records, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can significantly streamline these processes by automating data entry, retrieval, and organization, reducing the need for human intervention. The occupation's core tasks are squarely in the domain where AI tools are rapidly improving, leading to a high exposure score.

title: Statistical officers and related research support occupations
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or undergraduate degree in a social science, commerce, statistics or a related field is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Statistical officers and related research support occupations involve significant data analysis, report generation, and coordination tasks, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. Tier 2 (LLM+tools) and Tier 3 (multi-agent systems) can automate data processing, statistical analysis, and report generation, reducing the need for human involvement in routine tasks. The core tasks are predominantly digital and align with areas where AI is rapidly advancing, leading to high exposure.

title: Accounting technicians and bookkeepers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

Completion of a college program in accounting, bookkeeping or a related field or completion of two years (first level) of a recognized professional accounting program (e.g., Chartered Accounting, Certified General Accounting) or courses in accounting or bookkeeping combined with several years of experience as a financial or accounting clerk are required.
exposure_rationale: Accounting technicians and bookkeepers are highly exposed to AI due to the nature of their work, which involves routine information processing, data entry, and financial record-keeping. Tier 2 AI tools can already automate significant portions of these tasks, such as generating financial reports, reconciling accounts, and managing invoices. Tier 3 multi-agent systems could further automate end-to-end workflows, reducing the need for human intervention in routine tasks.

title: Insurance adjusters and claims examiners
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A bachelor's degree, college diploma or some post-secondary education or several years of experience as a clerk in the claims department or other general insurance experience is required.
Several years of on-the-job training and completion of insurance industry courses and training programs are required.
exposure_rationale: Insurance adjusters and claims examiners are highly exposed to AI due to the nature of their work, which involves routine information processing, analysis, and coordination tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can assist significantly with data analysis, report generation, and communication, while Tier 3 multi-agent systems can automate workflows like claim processing and document management. Human judgment and regulatory compliance remain important, but the core tasks are increasingly within AI's capabilities.

title: Insurance underwriters
education_req: Completion of secondary school and some general insurance experience or a bachelor's degree, college diploma or some post-secondary education is required.
Several years of on-the-job training and completion of insurance industry underwriting courses and training programs are required.
exposure_rationale: Insurance underwriters primarily engage in evaluating risks and determining policy terms, tasks that involve significant data analysis and decision-making based on established criteria. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with data processing and risk assessment, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine underwriting decisions and policy issuance. Human oversight remains important for complex cases, but the core tasks are highly exposed to AI-driven efficiencies and automation.

title: Assessors, business valuators and appraisers
education_req: Assessors require a college diploma in municipal assessment and accreditation with the municipal assessment association of the province.

Business valuators usually require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in accounting, business or commerce and accreditation with the Canadian Institute of Chartered Business Valuators based on successful completion of its business and security valuation program.
Appraisers require a college diploma or vocational training in the field of appraisal or several years of experience in a position related to the field of appraisal.
exposure_rationale: Assessors, business valuators, and appraisers engage in predominantly knowledge-based tasks such as evaluating financial data, market trends, and property values. AI tools can assist in data analysis, report generation, and even initial valuation assessments, which increases exposure. While human judgment and expertise remain crucial, especially for nuanced evaluations and client interactions, Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can significantly streamline and automate routine data processing and reporting tasks, raising the occupation's AI exposure level.

title: Administrative officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A university degree or college diploma in business or public administration may be required.
Experience in a senior clerical or executive secretarial position related to office administration is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Administrative officers are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks involving coordination, scheduling, communication, and routine decision-making, which are increasingly handled by LLM+tools and multi-agent systems. These systems can automate tasks such as managing schedules, processing documents, and handling routine communications, significantly impacting the role. While human judgment and oversight remain relevant, the digital nature of the work makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.

title: Property administrators
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required in all provinces and territories except for British Columbia.
A language proficiency index is required in British Columbia.
Property administrators must be a minimum of nineteen years of age in British Columbia.
exposure_rationale: Property administrators engage in tasks like managing leases, coordinating maintenance, and handling tenant communications, which are highly exposed to AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can automate scheduling, lease drafting, and communication with tenants, while Tier 3 systems could manage entire workflows such as tenant onboarding and maintenance requests. Human judgment and client trust remain important, but the administrative and coordination aspects are under significant AI pressure.

title: Payroll administrators
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of college or other courses in accounting, bookkeeping or payroll administration or experience in payroll administration is usually required.
Payroll association certification may be required.
exposure_rationale: Payroll administrators are highly exposed to AI due to the routine and digital nature of their tasks, such as processing payroll, managing employee records, and ensuring compliance with regulations. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can significantly automate these tasks by integrating with databases and financial systems, while Tier 3 multi-agent systems could potentially handle entire payroll workflows with minimal human intervention. The core tasks of this occupation are squarely in the domain where AI is rapidly improving, leading to a high exposure score.

title: Administrative assistants
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a one- or two-year college or other program for administrative assistants or secretaries or previous clerical experience is required.
exposure_rationale: Administrative assistants are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks involving scheduling, communication, and coordination, which are increasingly handled by LLM+tools and multi-agent systems. These systems can automate routine tasks such as managing calendars, drafting correspondence, and processing documents, reducing the need for human involvement in these areas. While human judgment and interpersonal skills are still relevant, the digital nature of the work makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.

title: Legal administrative assistants
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a one- or two-year college or other program for secretaries or legal secretaries is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Legal administrative assistants are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks involving document preparation, scheduling, and communication, which are well within the capabilities of LLMs and LLM+tools. Multi-agent systems can automate workflows such as drafting legal documents, managing calendars, and handling routine correspondence, significantly reducing the need for human involvement in these areas. While human judgment and client trust remain important, the digital nature of the work makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.

title: Medical administrative assistants
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a one- or two-year college program or other specialized courses for secretaries or medical secretaries is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Medical administrative assistants are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks involving scheduling, communication, and coordination, which are well within the capabilities of LLM+tools and multi-agent systems. While human judgment and interpersonal skills are still relevant, the digital nature of the work makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.

title: Customs, ship and other brokers

A customs broker licence, issued by the Canada Border Services Agency, is required for an individual or company to operate a customs brokerage business.
exposure_rationale: Customs, ship, and other brokers primarily engage in coordination, communication, and documentation tasks, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. Tier 2 AI tools can streamline scheduling, document processing, and communication with clients and customs authorities. Tier 3 multi-agent systems could further automate workflows like client intake, documentation preparation, and compliance checks, reducing the need for human intervention in routine tasks.

education_req: Completion of a post-secondary education program of less than two years in Business or Supply Chain Management or extensive experience as a dispatcher, production clerk or scheduling clerk is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Production and transportation logistics coordinators are highly exposed to AI due to their focus on coordination, scheduling, and communication tasks, which are increasingly handled by AI tools and multi-agent systems. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can automate significant portions of logistics planning, inventory management, and transportation scheduling, reducing the need for human intervention. While human oversight and decision-making remain relevant, the core tasks are squarely in the domain where AI is rapidly improving.

title: General office support workers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of secondary school or college business or commercial courses is usually required.
exposure_rationale: General office support workers are highly exposed to AI due to the predominantly digital nature of their tasks, which include scheduling, communication, and routine decision-making. Tier 2 (LLM+tools) and Tier 3 (multi-agent systems) can significantly automate these tasks, such as managing correspondence, scheduling appointments, and processing documents. While human oversight and judgment are still relevant, the core tasks are squarely in the domain where AI is rapidly improving, leading to a high exposure score.

title: Personnel clerks

The core tasks of personnel clerks align closely with areas where AI is rapidly advancing, leading to a high exposure score.

While human judgment and legal accountability remain important, the potential for AI to streamline administrative processes is substantial, driving a high exposure score.

education_req: Completion of secondary school may be required.

exposure_rationale: Survey interviewers and statistical clerks primarily engage in data collection, entry, and analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. Tier 2 AI tools can handle data processing, entry, and initial analysis, while Tier 3 systems can automate entire workflows of survey administration and statistical reporting. The core tasks are digital and repetitive, making them highly exposed to AI advancements.

title: Data entry clerks
education_req: Completion of secondary school may be required.
College or other courses in data entry may be required.
exposure_rationale: Data entry clerks are highly exposed to AI as their core tasks involve routine information processing, which can be fully automated by multi-agent systems. These systems can handle the entire workflow of data entry with near-zero human involvement, making this occupation highly susceptible to AI-driven automation.

education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

exposure_rationale: Accounting and related clerks primarily engage in routine information processing, data entry, and coordination tasks, which are highly susceptible to automation by AI. The core tasks are digital and repetitive, making this occupation highly exposed to AI advancements.

exposure_rationale: Banking, insurance, and other financial clerks primarily engage in routine information processing, data entry, and coordination tasks, which are highly susceptible to automation by AI. education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

title: Library assistants and clerks
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Library assistants and clerks are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks involving information processing, cataloging, and routine administrative duties. Tier 2 AI tools can handle tasks like catalog management, information retrieval, and user queries, while Tier 3 systems could automate entire workflows such as book checkouts and returns. Tier 2 AI systems can automate tasks like order processing, inventory tracking, and supplier communication, while Tier 3 systems can manage entire workflows such as demand forecasting and procurement scheduling. exposure_rationale: Dispatchers primarily engage in coordination, scheduling, and communication tasks, which are highly susceptible to automation by AI tools. education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

Tier 2 AI tools can automate data analysis and report generation, while Tier 3 systems could potentially handle routine experimental design and result interpretation. exposure_rationale: Geoscientists and oceanographers engage in significant fieldwork and data collection, which are less susceptible to AI automation. While Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in data processing and report generation, the need for human judgment and interpretation in complex environmental contexts keeps AI exposure moderate.

title: Chemical engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in chemical engineering or in a related engineering discipline is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Chemical engineers engage in both hands-on experimental work and complex problem-solving, which involves significant knowledge and coordination tasks. AI can assist with data analysis, simulations, and optimizing processes, but human expertise in design and safety remains crucial. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity, but Tier 3 automation is less likely to fully replace the nuanced decision-making and innovation required in this field.

title: Industrial and manufacturing engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in industrial engineering or in a related engineering discipline is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Industrial and manufacturing engineers engage in both physical and digital tasks, including designing systems, optimizing processes, and improving production efficiency. AI can assist in data analysis, process simulation, and optimization, but human judgment and on-site presence remain crucial for implementation and troubleshooting. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in data-driven tasks, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the need for human oversight and decision-making in complex engineering environments.

title: Metallurgical and materials engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in metallurgical, materials, ceramic or chemical engineering or in a related engineering discipline is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Metallurgical and materials engineers engage in a mix of physical and analytical tasks, including designing materials and processes, which require human judgment and expertise. While AI can assist with data analysis and simulations, the core engineering tasks involve significant hands-on experimentation and problem-solving that are less susceptible to full automation. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance productivity in data handling and reporting, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced engineering work involved.

title: Mining engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in mining engineering or in a related engineering discipline is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Mining engineers have a moderate AI exposure as their work involves a blend of fieldwork, technical design, and coordination tasks. While AI can assist in data analysis, simulation, and project management, the physical and unpredictable nature of mining operations requires human judgment and presence. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance productivity in planning and reporting, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the human expertise needed in this field.

title: Geological engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in geological engineering or in a related discipline is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Geological engineers engage in a mix of fieldwork, data analysis, and project coordination. While AI can assist with data analysis and modeling, the core tasks require significant human judgment and field expertise. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline data processing and reporting, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the complex decision-making and on-site assessments integral to this role.

title: Petroleum engineers
exposure_rationale: Petroleum engineers engage in a mix of field work and technical analysis, with AI potentially assisting in data analysis, simulation, and optimization tasks. While AI can enhance productivity in data-driven aspects, the hands-on and decision-making components in unpredictable environments limit full automation. Tier 2 tools may aid in report generation and data processing, but Tier 3 multi-agent systems are less applicable due to the complex, non-routine nature of the work.

title: Aerospace engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in aerospace engineering or in a related engineering discipline, such as mechanical engineering or engineering physics, is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Aerospace engineers engage in complex design, analysis, and problem-solving tasks that require significant human judgment and expertise. While AI can assist with simulations, data analysis, and drafting, the core engineering tasks still rely heavily on human oversight and creativity. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the nuanced decision-making and innovative aspects of this role.

title: Other professional engineers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in an appropriate engineering discipline is required.
A master's degree or doctorate in a related engineering discipline may be required.
Licensing by a provincial or territorial association of professional engineers is required to approve engineering drawings and reports, and to practise as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.).
exposure_rationale: Other professional engineers engage in a mix of technical, analytical, and coordination tasks. While AI can assist in data analysis and design tasks, human judgment and expertise remain crucial for complex problem-solving and decision-making. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity by automating routine data processing and report generation, but Tier 3 systems are less likely to fully automate the nuanced engineering tasks that require deep domain knowledge and creativity.

title: Chemical technologists and technicians
education_req: Chemical technologists usually require completion of a two- or three-year college program in chemical, biochemical or chemical engineering technology or a closely related discipline.
Chemical technicians usually require completion of a one- or two-year college program in chemical, biochemical or chemical engineering technology.
National certification for chemical technologists and technicians is available through the Canadian Society for Chemical Technology.
exposure_rationale: Chemical technologists and technicians perform a mix of laboratory work, data analysis, and reporting. AI can assist with data analysis and report generation, but the hands-on laboratory tasks and the need for human judgment in experimental procedures limit full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity by handling data processing and documentation, but Tier 3 systems are unlikely to fully automate the core experimental tasks.

title: Geological and mineral technologists and technicians
education_req: Geological and mineral technologists usually require completion of a two- to three-year college program in geological technology, petroleum technology, petroleum engineering technology, hydrogeology or groundwater technology, mining technology, mining engineering technology, mineralogy, metallurgical technology, or welding technology.
Geophysics technologists usually require completion of a two- to three-year college program in electronics technology.
Geological and mineral technicians usually require completion of a one- to two-year college program in a related field.
exposure_rationale: Geological and mineral technologists and technicians perform a mix of fieldwork and data analysis. While AI can assist with data processing and analysis tasks, the hands-on and site-specific nature of geological work limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance data interpretation and reporting, but human judgment and field presence remain crucial, keeping exposure moderate.

title: Biological technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- to three-year college program in a field related to agriculture, biology, microbiology, wildlife or resource management is usually required for employment as a biological technologist.
Completion of a one- to two-year college program in a related field is required for employment as a biological technician.
Certification with provincial associations is available, but voluntary.
exposure_rationale: Biological technologists and technicians engage in both laboratory work and data analysis, which involves a blend of physical and cognitive tasks. AI can assist with data analysis, report generation, and some routine experimental procedures. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity by automating data processing and documentation tasks, but human oversight and expertise in experimental design and interpretation remain crucial. The role's exposure is moderate due to the combination of hands-on lab work and digital data handling.

title: Agricultural and fish products inspectors
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Government inspectors usually require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in agriculture, biology, chemistry, food processing technology or a related discipline.
Inspectors (other than government) may require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in a related discipline.
exposure_rationale: Agricultural and fish products inspectors primarily engage in physical inspections and assessments of products, which require human sensory evaluation and judgment. While AI can assist with data analysis and reporting, the core tasks are hands-on and require physical presence. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in documentation and scheduling, but the primary value remains in the manual inspection and decision-making process.

title: Forestry technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a one- to three-year college program in forestry technology or in a renewable resource program or forest ranger program is usually required.
Registration with a regulatory body is required for forestry technologists or technicians in all provinces except Prince Edward Island and Manitoba.
Certification or licensing as a scaler is required for some positions.
exposure_rationale: Forestry technologists and technicians engage in a mix of fieldwork and data analysis, including tasks like surveying, mapping, and environmental monitoring. While AI can assist with data processing and analysis, the physical and environmental aspects of the job require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in data management and reporting, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the human elements involved in field assessments and ecological evaluations.

title: Conservation and fishery officers
exposure_rationale: Conservation and fishery officers primarily engage in fieldwork, enforcement, and monitoring activities that require physical presence and human judgment. While AI can assist with data analysis and reporting, the core tasks involve unpredictable environments and interpersonal interactions, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in administrative tasks, but the overall impact on the occupation remains low due to the hands-on nature of the work.

title: Landscape and horticulture technicians and specialists
education_req: Completion of a two-to-three year college program in agronomy, arboriculture, horticulture, landscaping, landscape design or landscape technology is usually required.
Experience as a landscape and grounds maintenance labourer may be required for golf course superintendents, landscape gardeners and landscapers.
An apprenticeship program is available for horticulturists, arboriculturists and landscape gardeners.
exposure_rationale: Landscape and horticulture technicians and specialists primarily engage in physical and hands-on work related to plant care, landscape design, and maintenance. While AI can assist with design software and scheduling, the core tasks require manual skill and presence. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in planning and administrative tasks, but the dominant value remains in physical execution and environmental interaction, limiting cognitive AI exposure.

title: Architectural technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- to three-year college program in architectural technology or a related subject is usually required.

Certification in architectural technology or in a related field through provincial associations of architectural or engineering/applied science technologists and technicians may be required by employers.
A period of supervised work experience, usually two years, is required before certification.
exposure_rationale: Architectural technologists and technicians engage in tasks such as drafting, designing, and analyzing architectural plans, which are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI systems can assist with design software, automate routine calculations, and enhance drafting precision. While human judgment and creativity remain crucial, Tier 3 systems could further automate coordination and routine decision-making in project management, increasing exposure significantly.

title: Industrial designers
education_req: A university degree in industrial design, architecture, engineering or a college diploma in industrial design is required.
Creative ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, is required.
exposure_rationale: Industrial designers engage in tasks such as conceptualizing and designing products, which involve significant knowledge work and coordination. AI tools can assist with drafting, modelling, and iterative design processes, enhancing productivity. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can streamline communication and project management, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine design iterations and client feedback loops, increasing exposure to AI-driven changes.

title: Drafting technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a two- to three-year college program in engineering design and drafting technology or in a related field is usually required for drafting and design technologists.

Completion of a one- to two-year college program in drafting or completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program or four to five years of related experience plus completion of college or industry courses in drafting are usually required for drafting technicians.
exposure_rationale: Drafting technologists and technicians primarily engage in digital tasks such as creating technical drawings and plans using CAD software, which are highly susceptible to AI advancements. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity by automating routine design tasks and generating drafts from specifications. Tier 3 systems could further automate workflows by integrating design revisions and client feedback, reducing the need for human intervention in routine coordination tasks.

title: Land survey technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a two- to three-year college program in geomatics or land survey technology is usually required for land survey technologists.
Completion of a one- to two-year college program in geomatics or land survey technology is usually required for land survey technicians.
exposure_rationale: Land survey technologists and technicians perform a mix of fieldwork and data processing tasks. While AI can assist with data analysis and mapping, the physical and on-site nature of surveying limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance data processing and reporting, but human judgment and presence remain crucial for interpreting results and managing equipment in diverse terrains.

title: Technical occupations in geomatics and meteorology
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

Geomatics technologists and technicians require completion of a college program in geomatics, cartography, photogrammetry, aerial survey, remote sensing, geographic information system (GIS) or geomatics.
In Quebec, membership in the regulatory body for professional technologists is required to use the title ''Professional Technologist''.
exposure_rationale: Technical occupations in geomatics and meteorology involve a mix of data analysis, mapping, and technical support tasks. AI can assist in data processing, pattern recognition, and predictive modeling, which are core to these roles. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity by automating data analysis and report generation, while Tier 3 systems could further streamline workflows. However, human judgment and field-specific expertise remain crucial, moderating the overall AI exposure.

title: Computer network and web technicians
education_req: Completion of a college or other program in computer science, network administration, Web technology or a related field is usually required.
Certification or training provided by software vendors may be required by some employers.
Registration with a regulatory body is required in Saskatchewan.
exposure_rationale: Computer network and web technicians are exposed to AI through tasks like network monitoring, troubleshooting, and system optimization, which can be enhanced by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist in automating routine diagnostics and maintenance tasks, while Tier 3 systems could further streamline network management and configuration. However, human oversight and problem-solving remain crucial, moderating the overall exposure.

title: User support technicians
education_req: Completion of a college program in computer science, computer programming or network administration is usually required.
College or other courses in computer programming or network administration are usually required.
Certification or training provided by software vendors may be required by some employers.
exposure_rationale: User support technicians are highly exposed to AI as their core tasks involve troubleshooting, diagnosing, and resolving technical issues, which can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can automate routine diagnostics, provide instant solutions, and manage support tickets, while Tier 3 systems could potentially handle entire support workflows. Human judgment and interpersonal skills remain relevant for complex issues and customer interactions, but the potential for AI-driven efficiency gains is substantial.

title: Information systems testing technicians
education_req: Completion of a college program in computer science, computer programming or network administration is usually required.
College or other courses in computer programming or network administration are usually required.
Certification or training provided by software vendors may be required by some employers.
exposure_rationale: Information systems testing technicians are highly exposed to AI as their work involves significant digital and analytical tasks. AI tools can automate many testing processes, including bug detection, regression testing, and performance analysis, which are core tasks for these technicians. While human oversight and judgment are still necessary, especially for complex or novel systems, Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can significantly enhance productivity and automate routine testing workflows.

title: Non-destructive testers and inspectors
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of two years of an approved post-secondary science or engineering program may be required.
Additional classroom and on-the-job training is required for certification in non-destructive testing.
exposure_rationale: Non-destructive testers and inspectors primarily engage in physical inspection and testing of materials and structures, which involves hands-on work that is not easily automated by AI. While AI can assist in data analysis and report generation, the core tasks require human judgment and physical presence. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have limited impact on this occupation as the primary value lies in manual skill and expertise in physical testing methods.

title: Engineering inspectors and regulatory officers
education_req: University degree or college diploma in an appropriate engineering field or trade qualifications and extensive related work experience are required.
Appropriate professional engineering or engineering technology certification and licences may be required.
exposure_rationale: Engineering inspectors and regulatory officers perform tasks that involve both physical inspections and regulatory compliance, which require human judgment and presence. While AI can assist with data analysis and report generation, the core tasks of physical inspection and regulatory decision-making remain human-centric. Tier 2 AI tools may support documentation and coordination tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the nuanced and situational aspects of this role.

title: Occupational health and safety specialists
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in occupational health and safety is usually required.
Occupational health and safety officers may require certification with the Board of Canadian Registered Safety Professionals (BCRSP).
exposure_rationale: Occupational health and safety specialists engage in both fieldwork and administrative tasks, such as conducting safety audits, preparing reports, and ensuring compliance with regulations. AI can assist with data analysis, report generation, and compliance tracking, but human judgment and on-site evaluations remain crucial. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in documentation and communication, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the need for human oversight and decision-making in safety assessments.

title: Construction inspectors
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

A college diploma in construction, civil engineering or architectural technology plus several years of related work experience or several years of experience as a qualified tradesperson in a construction trade, such as plumbing, carpentry or electrical trade are usually required.
Provincial certification in a skilled trade or as an engineering technologist is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Construction inspectors have a moderate AI exposure as their role involves both physical site inspections and administrative tasks like report writing and compliance checks. While AI can assist with data analysis and documentation, the need for on-site presence and human judgment in assessing construction quality and safety limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline reporting and data management, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making required on-site.

title: Civil engineering technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in civil engineering technology or a closely related discipline is usually required for civil engineering technologists.
Completion of a one- or two-year college program in civil engineering technology is usually required for civil engineering technicians.

Certification in civil engineering technology or in a related field is available through provincial associations of engineering/applied science technologists and technicians and may be required for some positions.
exposure_rationale: Civil engineering technologists and technicians perform a mix of fieldwork and technical tasks such as drafting, design, and data analysis. While AI can assist with design and analysis tasks, human judgment and on-site presence remain crucial. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in drafting and data management, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the need for human oversight and fieldwork integration.

title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in mechanical engineering technology is usually required for mechanical engineering technologists.
Completion of a one- or two-year college program in mechanical engineering technology is usually required for mechanical engineering technicians.

Certification in mechanical engineering technology or in a related field is available through provincial associations of engineering/applied science technologists and technicians and may be required for some positions.
exposure_rationale: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians engage in both physical and digital tasks, including design, testing, and analysis. While AI can assist with design simulations and data analysis, the hands-on nature of prototyping and testing limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in documentation and reporting, but human oversight and technical expertise remain crucial, keeping exposure moderate.

title: Industrial engineering and manufacturing technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- or three-year college program or equivalent in industrial engineering technology, pulp and paper technology, plastics technology, textile technology, manufacturing technology or a related discipline is usually required for industrial engineering or manufacturing technologists.
Completion of a one- or two-year college program in industrial engineering technology or in a related discipline is usually required for industrial engineering or manufacturing technicians.

Certification in industrial engineering or manufacturing technology or in a related field is available through provincial or territorial associations of engineering/applied science technologists and technicians and may be required for some positions.
exposure_rationale: Industrial engineering and manufacturing technologists and technicians engage in both physical and cognitive tasks, including the design, analysis, and optimization of manufacturing processes. While AI can assist with data analysis and process optimization, human oversight and judgment remain crucial. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity through data management and reporting, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the need for on-site problem-solving and adaptation to physical environments.

title: Construction estimators
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

Completion of a three-year college program in civil or construction engineering technology or several years of experience as a qualified tradesperson in a construction trade such as plumbing, carpentry or electrical, are required.
Certification by the Canadian Institute of Quantity Surveyors is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Construction estimators primarily engage in knowledge work involving the analysis of project specifications, cost estimation, and coordination with various stakeholders. AI tools can assist in data analysis, cost estimation, and report generation, enhancing productivity. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can automate significant portions of the estimation process, such as data retrieval and preliminary calculations, but human judgment and expertise remain crucial for final decision-making and adapting to unique project conditions.

title: Electrical and electronics engineering technologists and technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in electrical or electronics engineering technology, computer engineering technology, telecommunications technology or an equivalent is usually required for electrical or electronics engineering technologists.
Completion of a one- or two-year college program in electrical or electronics engineering technology is usually required for electrical or electronics engineering technicians.

Certification in electrical or electronics engineering technology or in a related field is available through provincial associations of engineering/applied science technologists and technicians and may be required for some positions.
exposure_rationale: Electrical and electronics engineering technologists and technicians perform a mix of hands-on technical tasks and knowledge work, such as testing, troubleshooting, and maintaining electronic systems. While AI can assist with data analysis and documentation, the physical and technical nature of the work limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity by aiding in diagnostics and reporting, but human expertise and presence remain crucial for complex problem-solving and equipment handling.

title: Electronic service technicians (household and business equipment)
education_req: Completion of a two- to three-year college program in electronics or completion of a four-year apprenticeship program in electronic servicing and repair or completion of high school or college courses in electronics and on-the-job training is required.
Trade certification for electronics technicians (consumer products) is available, but voluntary, in Ontario, British Columbia and the Yukon.
exposure_rationale: Electronic service technicians primarily engage in hands-on repair and maintenance of household and business equipment, which involves physical tasks that are not easily automated by AI. While AI may assist in diagnostics or provide technical support, the core value of the job remains in manual skill and physical presence. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have limited impact on the primary tasks of this occupation, keeping the AI exposure low.

title: Industrial instrument technicians and mechanics
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.

Completion of a four- or five-year apprenticeship program in industrial instrument repair or completion of a two-year college program in industrial instrumentation technology and several years of work experience are usually required for trade certification.
Instrumentation and control technician trade certification is available, but voluntary, in all provinces and territories, except in Quebec.
exposure_rationale: Industrial instrument technicians and mechanics primarily engage in hands-on technical work involving the installation, maintenance, and repair of industrial instruments and control systems.
