While AI can assist with diagnostics and data analysis, the core tasks require physical presence and manual skill. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in documentation and scheduling, but the dominant value remains in the physical and technical expertise required on-site.

title: Aircraft instrument, electrical and avionics mechanics, technicians and inspectors
education_req: Aircraft instrument and avionics mechanics and technicians require completion of a two- to three-year college program in avionics or electronics.
Aircraft electrical mechanics and technicians require completion of a college program as an electrical or electronic technician.
Four years of on-the-job or company apprenticeship training is required for all mechanics and technicians in this unit group.
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of aircraft instrument, electrical and avionics mechanics, technicians, and inspectors are predominantly hands-on and require physical presence and technical skill. While AI can assist with diagnostics and documentation, the primary value of this occupation lies in manual expertise and safety-critical inspections, which are less susceptible to cognitive AI automation. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in administrative tasks, but the overall exposure remains low due to the necessity of human judgment and physical intervention.

title: Managers in health care
education_req: Administrators of departments such as surgery, clinical medicine or laboratory medicine are usually required to have the education and training of a specialist physician.
Administrators of other departments, such as nursing, dietetics or physiotherapy, are usually required to have the education and training of the medical professionals in that department.
Several years of experience in the relevant profession, including supervisory experience, are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Managers in health care primarily engage in coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks, which are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist with scheduling, report generation, and data analysis, enhancing productivity. However, human judgment and regulatory accountability remain crucial, limiting full automation potential. The role's exposure is high due to the significant cognitive and coordination elements, but not at the maximum level due to the need for human oversight and decision-making in health care contexts.

title: Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine
education_req: A bachelor's degree or in Quebec, completion of a college program and one year of pre-medicine university studies is usually required.
Graduation from an approved medical school and specific specialty training are required.
Completion of the certifying examinations of the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada and licensing by the provincial or territorial licensing authority are required.
exposure_rationale: Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine have a moderate AI exposure. While AI can assist with data analysis, diagnostics, and administrative tasks, the core of their work involves hands-on clinical judgment and patient interaction, which remain less susceptible to full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance efficiency in routine tasks, but Tier 3 multi-agent systems are unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and human presence required in this field.

title: Specialists in surgery
exposure_rationale: Specialists in surgery primarily engage in hands-on clinical work that requires physical presence and advanced manual skills, which are not easily replaced by AI. While AI can assist with diagnostics and administrative tasks, the core surgical tasks remain largely unaffected by cognitive AI advancements. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in scheduling and patient record management, but the primary value of this occupation is in the physical execution of surgical procedures.

title: General practitioners and family physicians
education_req: A bachelor's degree or in Quebec, completion of a college program and one year of pre-medicine university studies is usually required.
Graduation from an approved medical school and two to three years of family medicine residency training are required.
Completion of the qualifying examinations of the Medical Council of Canada or the Collège des médecins du Québec and licensing by the provincial or territorial licensing authority are required.
exposure_rationale: General practitioners and family physicians have moderate AI exposure as AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling, record-keeping, and preliminary diagnosis support. However, the core tasks of diagnosing, treating, and patient interaction require human judgment and empathy, limiting full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can aid in efficiency, but Tier 3 systems are unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and interpersonal aspects of the role.

title: Veterinarians
exposure_rationale: Veterinarians perform a blend of hands-on clinical work and knowledge-based tasks such as diagnosing and treating animals. While AI can assist with diagnostic tools and administrative tasks, the core clinical and judgment-based aspects of veterinary work require human expertise and presence. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative duties, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to significantly impact the core clinical tasks in the near term.

title: Dentists
exposure_rationale: Dentists primarily perform hands-on clinical work that requires physical presence and manual skill, such as diagnosing and treating dental conditions. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core value of the occupation remains in the physical and interpersonal domain. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance efficiency in administrative tasks, but they do not significantly alter the core clinical responsibilities of dentists.

title: Optometrists
exposure_rationale: Optometrists perform a blend of clinical tasks and patient interactions that require human judgment and presence. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core diagnostic and treatment functions are less susceptible to automation. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some coordination tasks, but the physical and interpersonal nature of the work keeps AI exposure moderate.

title: Audiologists and speech-language pathologists
exposure_rationale: Audiologists and speech-language pathologists perform a blend of interpersonal clinical work and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with scheduling, documentation, and data analysis, the core diagnostic and therapeutic interactions require human judgment and presence. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative duties, but the primary value remains in-person patient care and expertise.

title: Pharmacists
exposure_rationale: Pharmacists perform a mix of clinical and administrative tasks, including dispensing medications, advising patients, and managing prescriptions. AI can assist with drug interaction checks, prescription management, and patient communication, but human judgment and patient trust remain crucial. Tier 2 AI tools can streamline administrative tasks, but the core clinical role limits full automation potential.

title: Dietitians and nutritionists
education_req: Dietitians require a bachelor's degree in dietetics, nutrition or a related field such as food and nutritional science from a program accredited by the Partnership for Dietetic Education and Practice (PDEP), and a period of supervised practical training.
Successful completion of the Canadian Dietetic Registration Exam (CDRE) is required in all provinces except Quebec.
Registration with a regulatory body is required in all provinces for dietitians.
exposure_rationale: Dietitians and nutritionists engage in a mix of interpersonal client consultations and knowledge-based tasks such as dietary planning and health promotion. AI can assist with data analysis, dietary recommendations, and administrative tasks, but human judgment and personalized client interaction remain central. Tier 2 AI tools could streamline scheduling and report generation, but Tier 3 automation is less likely to fully replace the nuanced, client-focused aspects of the role.

title: Psychologists
education_req: A doctoral degree in psychology is required in order to use the designation Psychologist in Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba and British Columbia.
A master's degree in psychology is required for the designation Psychologist in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Saskatchewan, Alberta, the Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
A master's degree in psychology is required in order to use the designation Psychological Associate in Prince Edward Island, Ontario, Manitoba and British Columbia.
exposure_rationale: Psychologists primarily engage in interpersonal and clinical tasks that require human judgment, empathy, and nuanced understanding of mental health, which are less susceptible to AI automation. However, AI tools can assist with administrative tasks, data analysis, and potentially augment diagnostic processes, indicating moderate exposure. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some coordination and documentation tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the core therapeutic and diagnostic functions of psychologists.

title: Chiropractors
exposure_rationale: Chiropractors primarily engage in hands-on clinical work that involves diagnosing and treating patients through physical manipulation, which is not easily automated by AI. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core value of the occupation remains in the physical and interpersonal domain. Thus, the cognitive AI exposure is low, though there may be some minor efficiency gains from Tier 2 AI tools.

title: Physiotherapists
exposure_rationale: Physiotherapists have a moderate AI exposure as their work involves a blend of physical, interpersonal, and knowledge-based tasks. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core value of physiotherapy lies in hands-on patient care and personalized treatment plans, which require human judgment and physical presence. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance efficiency in administrative areas, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the human-centric aspects of the role.

title: Occupational therapists
education_req: A university degree in occupational therapy including supervised fieldwork is required.
Completion of a national or provincial certification examination is required.
Licensure with a regulatory body is required in all provinces and territories.
exposure_rationale: Occupational therapists engage in a blend of physical, interpersonal, and knowledge work, focusing on diagnosing and treating patients. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and documentation, the core therapeutic and diagnostic tasks require human judgment and interaction. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some coordination tasks, but the human-centric nature of therapy limits full automation potential.

title: Kinesiologists and other professional occupations in therapy and assessment
education_req: Kinesiologists require a four-year bachelor’s degree in kinesiology, physical education or human kinetics.
Certification for kinesiologists is available through the Canadian Kinesiology Alliance.
Registration with a regulatory body is required for kinesiologists in Ontario.
exposure_rationale: Kinesiologists and similar therapy professionals engage in a blend of physical, interpersonal, and knowledge work. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core value of the occupation involves hands-on assessment and treatment, which requires human judgment and interaction. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance efficiency, but the physical and personalized nature of the work limits full automation potential.

title: Other professional occupations in health diagnosing and treating
exposure_rationale: This occupation involves diagnosing and treating health conditions, which requires significant human judgment and interpersonal skills. While AI can assist with administrative tasks and data analysis, the core diagnostic and treatment functions rely on human expertise and patient interaction. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in scheduling and record-keeping, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and patient care aspects of these roles.

title: Nursing coordinators and supervisors
education_req: Completion of a university, college or other approved registered nursing, or registered psychiatric nursing, program is required.
Courses in management studies or a degree, diploma or certificate in management or administration may be required.

Registration with a regulatory body is required for registered nurses in all provinces and territories and for registered psychiatric nurses in Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, British Columbia and the Yukon.
exposure_rationale: Nursing coordinators and supervisors have a moderate exposure to AI as their roles involve a blend of administrative coordination and interpersonal healthcare tasks. AI can assist with scheduling, managing patient records, and generating reports, which are part of their duties. However, the need for human judgment, clinical decision-making, and direct patient interaction limits the extent of AI's impact, keeping exposure at a moderate level.

title: Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses
education_req: Registered nurses
Completion of a university, college or other approved registered nursing program is required.
Additional academic training or experience is required to specialize in a specific area of nursing.
exposure_rationale: Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses have a moderate level of AI exposure. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core responsibilities involve hands-on clinical care, patient interaction, and critical decision-making that require human judgment and empathy. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some coordination tasks, but the primary value of the role remains in physical and interpersonal skills.

title: Nurse practitioners
exposure_rationale: Nurse practitioners perform a blend of clinical and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with documentation, scheduling, and some diagnostic support, the core value of the role involves hands-on patient care and clinical judgment, which remain less exposed to AI. Tier 2 tools may streamline administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the human-centric aspects of the job.

title: Physician assistants, midwives and allied health professionals
education_req: Physician assistants
Physician assistants require completion of a university degree in a physician assistant program.
Physician assistants require registration with a regulatory body in Manitoba and New Brunswick.
exposure_rationale: The role involves a blend of clinical and administrative tasks, with AI potentially assisting in documentation, scheduling, and patient communication. However, the core value remains in direct patient care and clinical judgment, which are less susceptible to AI automation. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance efficiency in non-clinical tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the human-centric aspects of the job.

title: Opticians
education_req: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in ophthalmic dispensing or optical sciences is required.
Licensing by a regulatory body for opticians is required in all provinces.
Licensing requirements for scope of practice and protected titles related to the sale or dispensing and fitting of contact lenses vary by province.
exposure_rationale: Opticians primarily engage in hands-on clinical work, including fitting and adjusting eyewear, which requires physical presence and manual skill. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and inventory management, the core tasks remain largely manual and patient-facing. Thus, AI exposure is low, as the role's value is predominantly in physical and interpersonal skills rather than digital coordination.

title: Licensed practical nurses
education_req: Completion of a vocational, college or other approved program for licensed practical nurses is required.
Registration with a regulatory body is required in all provinces and territories.
Completion of a registration examination is required in all provinces and territories.
exposure_rationale: Licensed practical nurses perform a blend of hands-on clinical tasks and administrative duties. While AI can assist with scheduling, record-keeping, and some diagnostic support, the core value of the role involves physical presence and human judgment in patient care. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the essential human elements of the job.

title: Paramedical occupations
education_req: Completion of a one- to three-year college, hospital-based or other recognized paramedical or emergency medical technology program is required.
Licensing by a regulatory body is required in all provinces.
Those operating emergency vehicles require an appropriate class of licence.
exposure_rationale: Paramedical occupations involve diagnosing and treating illness and injury, which are primarily hands-on and require physical presence and human judgment. While AI can assist with some administrative tasks and data analysis, the core value of these roles is in physical and clinical skills, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools have minimal impact on the primary tasks of these occupations.

title: Respiratory therapists, clinical perfusionists and cardiopulmonary technologists
education_req: Respiratory therapists require the completion of a three-year college or university degree program in respiratory therapy, both including clinical training.
Licensing is required for respiratory therapists in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan and Alberta.
A national certification for respiratory therapists is available for therapists working in Prince Edward Island, British Columbia, Yukon, the Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of respiratory therapists, clinical perfusionists, and cardiopulmonary technologists involve hands-on clinical work and direct patient care, which are not easily automated by AI. While AI may assist with some diagnostic tools or administrative tasks, the primary value of these roles is in their physical and clinical expertise. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure remains low, despite potential advancements in AI-assisted diagnostics.

title: Animal health technologists and veterinary technicians
education_req: Completion of a two- or three-year animal health/veterinary technology college program is required.
Successful completion of a national registration examination may be required in some employment settings.
Registration with provincial animal health technologists' or veterinary technicians' associations is available, and is mandatory in some provinces.
exposure_rationale: Animal health technologists and veterinary technicians perform a blend of physical and knowledge-based tasks, including diagnosing and treating animals, which require human judgment and hands-on care. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core clinical and interpersonal aspects of the job remain less exposed to AI. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance efficiency in documentation and communication, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the human-centric elements of the role.

title: Other technical occupations in therapy and assessment
education_req: Hearing instrument practitioners require completion of a two or three-year college diploma in a hearing instrument specialist program, and a period of supervised training.

Licensure with a regulatory body is required for hearing instrument practitioners in all provinces except in Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, and Ontario. Ontario requires membership with the Association of Hearing Instrument Practitioners (AHIP). Hearing instrument practitioners can prescribe and dispense hearing instruments except in Ontario where dispensing is dependent on a prescription from a physician or audiologist.
Communicative disorders assistants and speech-language pathology assistants require completion of a three- to four-year college program.
exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a blend of technical, diagnostic, and therapeutic tasks in health care, which require human presence and judgment. While AI can assist with some diagnostic tools and administrative tasks, the core value remains in physical and interpersonal care. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in scheduling and documentation, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the nuanced human interaction required in therapy and assessment roles.

title: Denturists
exposure_rationale: Denturists primarily perform hands-on clinical work, including creating and fitting dentures, which requires manual skill and patient interaction. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling and record-keeping, the core value of the occupation is in the physical and clinical expertise. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure remains low, as the primary tasks are not easily automated by current AI technologies.

title: Dental hygienists and dental therapists
education_req: Completion of a two- to three-year college dental hygiene program, approved by the governing board within the province or territory of residence, is usually required for dental hygienists.
Dental therapists are required to complete a two- to three- year college diploma from an approved dental institution program.
Licensing by the appropriate provincial or territorial regulatory body is required for dental hygienists.
exposure_rationale: Dental hygienists and dental therapists primarily engage in hands-on clinical work, which involves diagnosing and treating oral health issues. While AI may assist with scheduling and minor administrative tasks, the core value of the occupation is in manual skill and direct patient interaction, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have minimal impact on the primary tasks of this occupation.

title: Dental technologists and technicians
education_req: Dental technologists and technicians are required to complete a college program in dental technology or four or more years of on-the-job training under the supervision of a registered dental technologist or technician.
Registration with a regulatory body is required for dental technologists and technicians in all provinces except in Manitoba and the territories.
exposure_rationale: Dental technologists and technicians primarily engage in hands-on work creating dental prosthetics and appliances, which requires manual skill and precision. While AI can assist with design and planning through digital tools, the core tasks remain largely physical and require human expertise. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have limited impact on this occupation as the primary value is in the manual fabrication and customization of dental products.

title: Medical laboratory technologists
education_req: A two- or three-year college program in medical laboratory technology is required and a period of supervised practical training may be required.
Registration with a regulatory body is required in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, and Alberta.
Certification by the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science is usually required by employers.
exposure_rationale: Medical laboratory technologists perform tasks that involve both technical skills and knowledge work, such as analyzing samples and operating laboratory equipment. While AI can assist with data analysis and result interpretation, human oversight and judgment remain crucial, especially in clinical settings. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative tasks, but the core diagnostic and technical work requires human expertise, limiting full automation potential.

title: Medical radiation technologists
education_req: Completion of a two- to three-year college or other approved program in diagnostic radiography or magnetic resonance imaging (for radiological technologists and magnetic resonance technologists), nuclear medicine technology (for nuclear medicine technologists) or radiation therapy (for radiation therapists) or a bachelor of health sciences in radiography, nuclear medicine or radiation therapy and a period of supervised practical training are required.
Magnetic resonance technologists require completion of a graduate certificate program in magnetic resonance imaging.
Licensure with a regulatory body is required in Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Saskatchewan and Alberta.
exposure_rationale: Medical radiation technologists perform tasks that involve both technical operation of imaging equipment and patient interaction, which are less susceptible to AI automation. While AI can assist in image analysis and administrative tasks, the core responsibilities require human judgment and patient care. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in scheduling and report generation, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the hands-on and interpretive aspects of the role.

title: Medical sonographers
education_req: Completion of a three- to four-year program in diagnostic medical sonography or ultrasound technology or completion of a two- to three-year program in a related allied health field and a one-year post-diploma program in diagnostic medical sonography from an accredited institution are required.
Registration with Sonography Canada and/or the American Registry of Diagnostic Medical Sonographers is usually required by employers.
Registration with l'Ordre des technologues en imagerie médicale, en radio-oncologie et en électrophysiologie médicale du Québec is required in Quebec.
exposure_rationale: Medical sonographers primarily perform hands-on diagnostic imaging tasks that require physical presence and expertise in operating specialized equipment. While AI can assist with image analysis and administrative tasks, the core value of the role remains in the physical and clinical skills required to conduct sonographic examinations. Thus, AI exposure is low, as the role is not predominantly digital or coordination-based.

title: Cardiology technologists and electrophysiological diagnostic technologists
education_req: Cardiology technologists require completion of a two-year college diploma program in cardiology technology and supervised practical training.
Cardiology technologists require registration with the Canadian Society of Cardiology Technologists in all provinces except Quebec.
Electroencephalograph and other diagnostic technologists require completion of a two-year college or hospital-based program in electrophysiology technology and supervised practical training.
exposure_rationale: Cardiology technologists and electrophysiological diagnostic technologists perform tasks that involve both technical skills and patient interaction, such as operating diagnostic equipment and interpreting results. While AI can assist in data analysis and improve diagnostic accuracy, the hands-on and patient-facing aspects of the job limit full automation. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance efficiency in data processing and reporting, but human judgment and interaction remain central to the role.

title: Pharmacy technicians
education_req: Pharmacy technicians usually require a two-year pharmacy technician college program or completion of a two- to three-year college program in pharmaceutics and supervised practical training.
Completion of the Pharmacy Examining Board of Canada (PEBC) certification exams is required for pharmacy technicians.
Registration with a regulatory body is required for pharmacy technicians in all provinces except Quebec.
exposure_rationale: Pharmacy technicians perform a mix of physical and cognitive tasks, including preparing medications, managing inventory, and maintaining records. While AI can assist with inventory management and record-keeping, the physical handling of medications and direct patient interactions require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the human elements of this role in the near term.

title: Other medical technologists and technicians
education_req: Dietary technicians require completion of a two- to three-year college program in dietary technology and supervised practical training.

Ocularists require completion of the education program of the American Society of Ocularists and supervised practical training or a five-year supervised practical training program under a certified ocularist.
Certification by the National Examining Board of Ocularists is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Other medical technologists and technicians perform tasks that blend technical skills with patient interaction and diagnostic work. While AI can assist with data analysis and administrative tasks, the hands-on clinical and diagnostic components require human judgment and presence. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some coordination tasks, but the core value remains in the physical and interpersonal aspects of the job.

title: Traditional Chinese medicine practitioners and acupuncturists
education_req: Traditional Chinese medicine practitioners usually require completion of a four to five-year diploma program in traditional Chinese medicine from a private institute or completion of a program in traditional Chinese medicine from recognized medical schools abroad.
Registration with a regulatory body is required for traditional Chinese medicine practitioners in Ontario and British Columbia, which includes completion of an approved registration examination.

Acupuncturists usually require completion of a three- to four-year diploma program in acupuncture from a private institute or completion of some university undergraduate studies in science and training in a related health care discipline or work experience under the supervision of a registered acupuncturist.
exposure_rationale: Traditional Chinese medicine practitioners and acupuncturists primarily engage in hands-on clinical work, diagnosing and treating patients through physical methods. While AI may assist with scheduling, patient records, and minor administrative tasks, the core value of the occupation lies in manual skill and patient interaction, which are less susceptible to cognitive AI automation.

title: Massage therapists
education_req: Massage therapists usually require completion of an 18- to 24-month or 18- to 36-month program in massage therapy from an accredited school and a period of supervised practical training.
Registration with a regulatory body is required in Newfoundland and Labrador, Ontario and British Columbia.
exposure_rationale: Massage therapists primarily engage in hands-on, physical work that involves diagnosing and treating physical conditions through manual techniques. While AI may assist with scheduling and minor administrative tasks, the core value of the occupation is the manual skill and physical presence required to perform massages. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure is low, with minimal impact from Tier 2 or Tier 3 AI systems.

title: Other practitioners of natural healing
education_req: Homeopaths usually require completion of a three-year diploma program in homeopathy.
Registration with a regulatory body is required for homeopaths in Ontario.
Other occupations in this unit group usually require completion of training programs specific to their type of practice and some supervised practical training.
exposure_rationale: Practitioners of natural healing primarily engage in hands-on, interpersonal work that involves diagnosing and treating patients, which is not easily replaced by AI. While AI may assist with scheduling, record-keeping, or providing supplementary information, the core tasks require human presence and judgment. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure remains low, though there may be some minor administrative efficiencies gained through AI tools.

title: Dental assistants and dental laboratory assistants
education_req: Dental assistants require completion of an eight-month to one-year college or other program in dental assistance or completion of secondary school with on-the-job training.
Registration with a regulatory body is required in all provinces except Quebec and Ontario.

Certification by the National Dental Assisting Examination Board is required to perform intra-oral duties in all provinces except in Quebec, where intra-oral duties are not performed by dental assistants.
exposure_rationale: Dental assistants and dental laboratory assistants primarily perform hands-on clinical tasks such as preparing patients, sterilizing instruments, and assisting dentists during procedures, which are not easily automated by AI. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling or record-keeping, the core value of the role remains in physical presence and manual skill. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure is low.

title: Medical laboratory assistants and related technical occupations
education_req: Medical laboratory assistants and related technical workers require completion of a college certificate program in medical laboratory science.
Certification by the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science is usually required by employers.
exposure_rationale: Medical laboratory assistants perform tasks such as specimen collection, preparation, and basic analysis, which involve both physical handling and technical skills. While AI can assist in data entry and result analysis, the core tasks require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the hands-on nature of the work.

title: Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates
education_req: Some secondary school education and on-the-job training or a personal support worker, nursing aide or health care aide college or private institutional program, or a college nursing orderly program and supervised practical training are required.
Completion of specialized courses such as cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) and first aid is usually required.
An appropriate class of driver's licence may be required for medical attendants.
exposure_rationale: Nurse aides, orderlies, and patient service associates primarily perform hands-on clinical tasks such as assisting with patient care, which are not easily automated by AI. While AI can assist with scheduling and minor administrative tasks, the core value of this occupation lies in physical presence and manual skill in a healthcare setting. Therefore, AI exposure remains low, despite potential physical automation risks in other areas of healthcare.

title: Pharmacy technical assistants and pharmacy assistants
education_req: Pharmacy technical assistants usually require completion of up to two-years of a Pharmacy Technical Assistance vocational program.
Pharmacy assistants usually require completion of a Pharmacy Assistant college program, ranging from six to twelve months or completion of secondary school and several months of on-the-job training.
exposure_rationale: Pharmacy technical assistants and pharmacy assistants perform tasks such as processing prescriptions, managing inventory, and assisting pharmacists, which involve a mix of physical and digital coordination tasks. AI tools can assist with inventory management and prescription processing, but human oversight and interaction remain crucial for patient safety and regulatory compliance. Tier 2 AI tools could streamline some administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the human elements of the role.

title: Other assisting occupations in support of health services
education_req: Audiometric technicians usually require a specialization in industrial audiometry testing.
Orthopedic technologists usually require completion of secondary school and several months of on-the-job training or a college orthopedic technologist program.
Registration with the Canadian Society of Orthopaedic Technologists is available and may be required by employers.
exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a mix of physical and administrative tasks in a healthcare setting. While AI can assist with scheduling, record-keeping, and some diagnostic support, the core value of these roles involves direct patient interaction and hands-on support, which limits AI exposure. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the human element required in these roles.

title: Government managers - health and social policy development and program administration
education_req: Government managers in health policy development and program administration
A bachelor's degree or college diploma in health science, hospital administration or public administration or a bachelor's degree in social science is required.
A graduate degree in a health, social science or an administrative discipline may be required.
exposure_rationale: Government managers in health and social policy development and program administration engage in significant knowledge work, coordination, and communication tasks. AI tools, particularly LLM+tools, can assist with drafting policy documents, analyzing data, and managing communication workflows. While human judgment and regulatory accountability remain crucial, multi-agent systems could automate routine administrative tasks, increasing AI exposure.

title: Government managers - economic analysis, policy development and program administration
education_req: A bachelor's degree in economics, business administration, commerce or public administration is required.
A master's degree in economics or a related field may be required.
Several years of experience as an economist, economic development officer or socio-economic researcher are required.
exposure_rationale: Government managers in economic analysis, policy development, and program administration engage in significant knowledge work, coordination, and communication tasks. AI tools, especially Tier 2 (LLM+tools), can assist in drafting reports, analyzing data, and managing communication workflows. While human judgment and regulatory accountability remain critical, multi-agent systems could automate routine coordination and administrative tasks, increasing exposure to AI advancements.

title: Government managers - education policy development and program administration
education_req: A bachelor's degree in education is required.
A master's degree in a particular educational specialization may be required.
Several years of combined experience as a school teacher and as a government educational policy researcher, consultant or program officer are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Government managers in education policy development and program administration engage in significant coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with drafting reports, scheduling meetings, and managing databases, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine policy analysis and program administration workflows. However, human judgment and regulatory accountability remain crucial, moderating the overall AI exposure.

title: Other managers in public administration
education_req: A bachelor's degree in a social science discipline, law or business administration is required.

Several years of experience in government policy development, research or program administration, or in a professional occupation in social science, law or business administration are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Managers in public administration engage in coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks that are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist with scheduling, report generation, and data analysis, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine administrative workflows. However, human judgment and regulatory accountability remain significant, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Administrators - post-secondary education and vocational training
education_req: Faculty administrators require a graduate degree in a field related to the academic faculty and several years of experience as a university professor or college teacher.
Registrars require an undergraduate degree in business administration or a related field and several years of experience in registration administration.
Administrators of vocational training schools usually require an undergraduate degree in business administration or expertise and certification in a subject of instruction.
exposure_rationale: Administrators in post-secondary education and vocational training are primarily involved in coordination, scheduling, and communication tasks, which are highly susceptible to AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can significantly assist with scheduling, managing databases, and processing routine communications. However, human judgment and interpersonal skills remain relevant for decision-making and complex problem-solving, which limits full automation potential.

title: School principals and administrators of elementary and secondary education
education_req: A bachelor's degree in education is required.
A master's degree in education may be required.
Several years of experience as a senior teacher or department head are required.
exposure_rationale: School principals and administrators engage in a mix of coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks, such as managing staff, developing policies, and overseeing school operations. While AI can assist with scheduling, data analysis, and routine communication, human judgment and leadership in educational settings remain crucial. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity, but Tier 3 systems are unlikely to fully automate the nuanced and interpersonal aspects of this role.

title: Managers in social, community and correctional services
education_req: Managers in social, community and correctional services usually require a master's degree in a social science or administrative discipline and several years of experience in a related occupation, such as a community and social service worker, social or health policy researcher, consultant or program officer, probation or parole officer, or social worker.
Managers of associations and membership organizations require extensive experience in a related occupation, trade or industry.
exposure_rationale: Managers in social, community, and correctional services engage in a mix of coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and report generation (Tier 2), the role requires significant human judgment and interpersonal skills, especially in managing teams and interacting with clients. Multi-agent systems (Tier 3) may streamline some workflows, but the core responsibilities remain human-centric, balancing the exposure to AI.

title: Commissioned police officers and related occupations in public protection services
exposure_rationale: Commissioned police officers and related occupations involve a blend of physical presence, interpersonal skills, and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with data analysis, report generation, and some coordination tasks, the core responsibilities require human judgment, decision-making, and physical presence. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in administrative efficiency, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the human-centric aspects of the role.

title: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers
exposure_rationale: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers primarily engage in leadership, strategic planning, and emergency response coordination, which are highly interpersonal and situational tasks. While AI can assist with data analysis and administrative tasks, the core responsibilities require human judgment and decision-making in unpredictable environments. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in scheduling and reporting, but the physical and leadership aspects of the role limit overall AI exposure.

title: Commissioned officers of the Canadian Armed Forces
education_req: A university degree is usually required.
Completion of military training is required.
Experience as a non-commissioned member of the armed forces and demonstrated leadership ability may substitute for a university degree.
exposure_rationale: Commissioned officers in the Canadian Armed Forces primarily engage in leadership, strategic planning, and decision-making roles that require human judgment and interpersonal skills. While AI can assist with administrative tasks and data analysis, the core responsibilities involve physical presence, leadership, and complex decision-making in dynamic environments, which are less susceptible to cognitive AI automation.

title: Judges
exposure_rationale: Judges primarily engage in decision-making, legal interpretation, and courtroom management, which are complex tasks requiring human judgment and understanding of nuanced legal contexts. While AI can assist with legal research and document analysis, the core responsibilities of judges involve significant human judgment and ethical considerations that are not easily automated. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may support administrative tasks, but the primary functions of judges remain less exposed to AI automation.

title: Lawyers and Quebec notaries
exposure_rationale: Lawyers and Quebec notaries engage in significant knowledge work, including legal research, document drafting, and client communication, which are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist with legal research, document management, and routine client interactions, while Tier 3 systems may automate more standardized legal processes. However, human judgment, client trust, and regulatory compliance remain critical, limiting full automation.

title: University professors and lecturers
education_req: A doctoral degree in the field of specialization is required for university professors.
A master's degree in the field of specialization is required for university lecturers.

Licences or professional certification may be required for professors teaching future practitioners in certain professionally regulated fields, such as medicine, engineering, architecture, psychology or law.
exposure_rationale: University professors and lecturers are primarily engaged in knowledge work, including teaching, research, and academic administration. AI tools can assist with grading, content creation, and research data analysis, enhancing productivity. However, human judgment, mentorship, and the interpersonal aspects of teaching remain central, limiting full automation. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can streamline administrative tasks, but the core educational and research functions still require significant human involvement.

title: Post-secondary teaching and research assistants
education_req: Enrolment in, or completion of, a university or college program is required.
exposure_rationale: Post-secondary teaching and research assistants are exposed to AI through tasks like grading, data analysis, and literature review, which can be enhanced by LLMs and LLM+tools. While human judgment and interaction remain important, AI can automate significant portions of research assistance and administrative tasks. Multi-agent systems could further streamline coordination and scheduling, increasing exposure relative to base-LLM capabilities.

title: College and other vocational instructors
education_req: A bachelor's degree, a college diploma or demonstrated expertise in the field of instruction is required.
A master's degree in the field of instruction may be required.
A certificate, diploma or degree in adult education may be required.
exposure_rationale: College and vocational instructors engage in knowledge dissemination, curriculum development, and student assessment, which are areas where AI can assist significantly. Tier 2 AI tools can aid in grading, content creation, and administrative tasks, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine instructional and scheduling tasks. However, human judgment, mentorship, and interpersonal interaction remain central to the role, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Secondary school teachers
education_req: Teachers of academic subjects require a bachelor's degree in education which is often preceded by a bachelor's degree in the arts or sciences.
Teachers of vocational or technical subjects require a bachelor's degree in education which is usually preceded by specialized training or experience in the subject.
Instructors of trades in Quebec require completion of an apprenticeship training program and industry or trade certification.
exposure_rationale: Secondary school teachers have a moderate AI exposure as their role involves a blend of interpersonal teaching and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with grading, lesson planning, and administrative coordination, the core teaching and student interaction require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools can streamline some tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the human elements of teaching in the near term.

title: Elementary school and kindergarten teachers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in education is required.
A bachelor's degree in child development may be required.
Additional training is required to specialize in special education or second language instruction.
exposure_rationale: Elementary school and kindergarten teachers have a moderate AI exposure as their core tasks involve a blend of interpersonal interaction, physical presence, and knowledge delivery. While AI can assist with lesson planning, grading, and administrative tasks, the essential human elements of teaching, such as classroom management and student engagement, remain central. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative duties, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced and interactive nature of teaching young children.

title: Social workers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in social work is required in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, British Columbia, the Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
In Alberta, a bachelor's degree or diploma in social work is required.
Supervised practical experience is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Social workers engage in a blend of interpersonal work and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with documentation, scheduling, and data management, the core of the job involves human interaction, empathy, and judgment, which are less susceptible to automation. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative tasks, but human presence and decision-making remain central to the role.

title: Therapists in counselling and related specialized therapies
education_req: A bachelor's or a master's degree in the field of counselling, therapy, mental health, psychology or a related social service discipline is required and a period of supervised clinical work with clients may be required.
A master’s degree in the field of therapy is required for art therapist, music therapist, and dance therapists.
Membership with a provincial or federal association is usually required by employers.
exposure_rationale: Therapists in counselling and related specialized therapies involve significant interpersonal interaction, emotional intelligence, and human judgment, which are less susceptible to AI automation. While AI tools can assist with scheduling, record-keeping, and some administrative tasks, the core therapeutic work remains human-centric. Tier 2 AI may streamline some coordination tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced and empathetic aspects of therapy.

title: Religious leaders
education_req: Completion of college, university or other program in religious studies is usually required. Some occupations in this unit group may require a master’s of divinity or theology.
A period of study with a senior minister is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Religious leaders primarily engage in interpersonal, community, and spiritual guidance activities that require human presence, empathy, and judgment. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and communication, the core value of this occupation is in-person interaction and leadership, which limits cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 tools may aid in minor administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to significantly impact the core responsibilities of religious leaders.

title: Police investigators and other investigative occupations
exposure_rationale: Police investigators and other investigative occupations involve a blend of fieldwork, interpersonal skills, and knowledge work. While AI can assist with data analysis, report generation, and some routine communication tasks, the core investigative work requires human judgment, intuition, and interaction with people, which limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and field presence required in these roles.

title: Probation and parole officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in social work, criminology, psychology, sociology or other related social science discipline is required.
A master's degree in social work, criminology, psychology or other related social science discipline may be required.
exposure_rationale: Probation and parole officers engage in a mix of interpersonal work and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with documentation, scheduling, and report generation, the core of the job involves human judgment, relationship-building, and decision-making in complex social contexts. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some administrative duties, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced human interactions required in this role.

title: Educational counsellors
education_req: A bachelor's degree in counselling, career development, education or social sciences is required for counsellors in school settings.
A master's degree in counselling psychology or a related field such as educational psychology, developmental psychology or social work is usually required.
Educational counsellors in post-secondary settings require an undergraduate degree and may require a graduate degree. A particular academic specialization may also be required.
exposure_rationale: Educational counsellors engage in both interpersonal work and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with scheduling, data management, and generating reports, the core of the job involves personal interaction, guidance, and judgment in educational contexts. Tier 2 AI tools can streamline administrative tasks, but human presence and empathy remain central to the role, limiting full automation potential.

title: Career development practitioners and career counsellors (except education)
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in employment counselling, career development or in a related field, such as human resources development, psychology, education or social services is required.
A master's degree in counselling psychology or a related field such as educational psychology, developmental psychology or social work may be required.
Vocational rehabilitation professional certification may be required by employers.
exposure_rationale: Career development practitioners and career counsellors engage in tasks such as advising clients, assessing skills, and developing career plans, which involve significant communication and coordination. AI tools can assist with data analysis, resume screening, and generating career advice, enhancing productivity. However, human judgment and interpersonal skills remain crucial, limiting full automation potential.

title: Natural and applied science policy researchers, consultants and program officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in a related scientific or technical discipline is usually required for occupations in this unit group.
A master's degree in a related scientific discipline may be required.

Certification through the Canadian College of Certified Professional Ergonomists is required to use the title Canadian Certified Professional Ergonomist (CCPE). Membership with the Association of Canadian Ergonomists may be required.
exposure_rationale: Natural and applied science policy researchers, consultants, and program officers engage in knowledge work involving research, analysis, and policy development. AI tools can assist significantly in data analysis, report generation, and drafting policy documents, which are core tasks of this occupation. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can automate parts of the research and coordination processes, but human judgment and expertise remain crucial for interpreting findings and making policy recommendations.

title: Economists and economic policy researchers and analysts
education_req: A master's degree in economics or in a related discipline such as business administration or statistics is usually required.
A doctorate in economics may be required.
exposure_rationale: Economists and economic policy researchers and analysts engage in tasks such as data analysis, report writing, and policy evaluation, which are highly susceptible to AI tools. Tier 2 AI can assist with data processing and report generation, while Tier 3 systems could automate workflows involving data collection, analysis, and initial policy drafting. Human judgment and interpretation remain crucial, but AI can significantly enhance productivity and efficiency in these roles.

title: Business development officers and market researchers and analysts
education_req: A bachelor's degree in economics, commerce, business administration or public administration is required.
Certification as a certified economic developer (Ec.D.) may be required.
Certification as a Certified Marketing Research Professional (CMRP) may be required.
exposure_rationale: Business development officers and market researchers and analysts engage in tasks such as data analysis, market research, and strategic planning, which are highly exposed to AI advancements. Tier 2 (LLM+tools) and Tier 3 (multi-agent systems) can significantly enhance or automate data gathering, analysis, and report generation, reducing the need for human intervention in routine tasks. However, human judgment and strategic decision-making remain important, preventing a higher score.

title: Social policy researchers, consultants and program officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in a social science or related discipline, or in business administration is usually required.
A master's degree in a social science or related discipline or in business administration may be required.
Home economists require a bachelor's degree in home economics, home economics education, human ecology, nutrition and food sciences or family and consumer studies.
exposure_rationale: Social policy researchers, consultants, and program officers engage in tasks such as data analysis, report writing, and policy development, which are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist significantly in data processing, report generation, and information synthesis. While human judgment and contextual understanding remain crucial, Tier 3 systems could automate routine data analysis and reporting tasks, increasing exposure to AI-driven efficiencies.

title: Health policy researchers, consultants and program officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in health science, public administration, recreation administration or hospital administration, or a bachelor's degree in social science, is required.
A graduate degree in health, social science or an administrative discipline may be required.
Several years of experience in the health field as a health care professional or as a community or social service worker are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Health policy researchers, consultants, and program officers engage in significant knowledge work involving data analysis, report generation, and policy evaluation, which are areas where AI, particularly LLMs and LLM+tools, can provide substantial assistance. While human judgment and expertise in policy interpretation remain crucial, AI can automate data processing, draft reports, and assist in research tasks, increasing exposure. Multi-agent systems could further streamline coordination and communication tasks, enhancing productivity but also raising exposure to AI-driven changes.

title: Education policy researchers, consultants and program officers
education_req: A bachelor's degree in education or in a discipline such as social science or business administration is required.
A master's degree in education may be required.
Specialized training or certification may be required.
exposure_rationale: Education policy researchers, consultants, and program officers engage in tasks such as data analysis, report writing, and policy development, which are susceptible to AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist significantly in data processing, drafting reports, and generating insights from databases, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine coordination tasks. However, human judgment and expertise in policy interpretation and stakeholder engagement remain crucial, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Recreation, sports and fitness policy researchers, consultants and program officers
education_req: A university degree in recreation administration, sports administration, physical education, kinesiology or related discipline combined with some work experience in a related field or a college diploma in a recreation, sports or fitness program combined with some experience in a related field is required.

Recreation, sports and fitness program supervisors require experience as program leaders or instructors in recreation, sports and fitness. Certification in program specialization, such as fitness or lifesaving, may also be required.
exposure_rationale: The role involves significant knowledge work, including policy research, consulting, and program management, which are areas where AI tools can enhance productivity. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist with data analysis, report generation, and communication tasks, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine coordination and scheduling tasks. However, human judgment and expertise in policy and program development remain crucial, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Program officers unique to government
exposure_rationale: Program officers in government roles often engage in coordination, communication, and administrative tasks, which are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI capabilities can handle scheduling, report generation, and routine correspondence, while Tier 3 systems could automate workflows involving data collection and processing. However, human judgment and regulatory accountability remain crucial, moderating full automation potential.

title: Other professional occupations in social science
education_req: A master's or doctoral degree in the discipline is usually required.
In Quebec, membership in the regulatory body for criminologists is required to use the title “Criminologist”.
exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a mix of interpersonal and knowledge-based tasks in fields like law, education, and social services. AI can assist with administrative tasks such as data analysis, report generation, and scheduling, but human judgment and interaction remain central. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity, but Tier 3 automation is less likely to fully replace the nuanced and context-specific decision-making required in these roles.

title: Police officers (except commissioned)
exposure_rationale: Police officers engage in a mix of physical, interpersonal, and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with report writing, data analysis, and some communication tasks, the core duties involve physical presence, judgment, and human interaction in unpredictable environments. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may streamline some administrative processes, but the primary role remains centered on human judgment and physical presence.

title: Firefighters
exposure_rationale: Firefighting is primarily a physical and unpredictable field occupation with minimal cognitive or digital tasks that AI could automate. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like reporting or scheduling, the core value of the job is in manual skill and physical presence during emergencies, which remain largely unaffected by AI advancements.

title: Specialized members of the Canadian Armed Forces
exposure_rationale: Specialized members of the Canadian Armed Forces primarily engage in roles that are physical, hands-on, and require human judgment and presence. While AI may assist with some administrative tasks, the core duties are not significantly impacted by cognitive AI tools. The nature of military operations and decision-making remains largely human-centric, limiting AI exposure to minor coordination tasks.

title: Paralegals and related occupations
education_req: Paralegals in law firms require a bachelor's degree in law or a law college diploma and in-house training from a law firm or other legal establishment.

Independent paralegals require knowledge of legal principles and procedures usually obtained through industry-sponsored courses and through experience, or through completion of a community college paralegal program.
Paralegals require a licence to perform advanced legal services.
exposure_rationale: Paralegals are highly exposed to AI due to their core tasks of document preparation, legal research, and case management, which are increasingly supported by LLMs and AI tools. Tier 2 and Tier 3 systems can automate significant portions of these tasks, such as drafting legal documents, managing schedules, and conducting research, reducing the need for human involvement in routine processes. While human judgment and client interaction remain important, the digital nature of the work makes it highly susceptible to AI advancements.

title: Social and community service workers
education_req: Completion of a college or university program in social work, child and youth care, psychology or other social science or health-related discipline is usually required.
Previous work experience in a social service environment as a volunteer or in a support capacity may replace formal education requirements for some occupations in this unit group.
Social service workers may be required to be a member of a provincial regulatory body in some provinces.
exposure_rationale: Social and community service workers engage in a mix of interpersonal support and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with scheduling, data entry, and report generation, the core of the job involves human interaction, empathy, and judgment, which are less susceptible to AI automation. Tier 2 AI tools may streamline some coordination tasks, but the human-centric nature of the work limits full automation potential.

title: Early childhood educators and assistants
education_req: Early childhood educators
Completion of a two- to four-year college program in early childhood education or a bachelor's degree in child development is required.

Licensing is required in Ontario and certification is required in British Columbia for early childhood educators. Licensing for early childhood educators is usually required in all other provinces and territories.
exposure_rationale: Early childhood educators and assistants primarily engage in hands-on, interpersonal work with children, which involves physical presence and human interaction that AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling or record-keeping, the core value of the role is in direct care and education, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have minimal impact on the core tasks of this occupation.

title: Instructors of persons with disabilities
title_jobbank: Special Education Technician - Persons With Disabilities
education_req: Completion of a college program in special education, rehabilitation, orientation and mobility, visual impairment, hearing impairment or intellectual disability is required.
A bachelor's degree in special education or a related field such as social work or psychology may be required.
exposure_rationale: Instructors of persons with disabilities engage in a blend of interpersonal, educational, and coordination tasks. While AI can assist with administrative duties and some educational content delivery, the core of the job involves personalized instruction and support that requires human empathy and adaptability. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in scheduling and resource management, but the human-centric nature of the work limits full automation potential.

title: Religion workers
education_req: Completion of college, university or other program in religious studies may be required, depending on the particular occupation and religion.
There may be other employment requirements depending on the particular occupation and religion.
exposure_rationale: Religion workers primarily engage in interpersonal, community-oriented tasks that require physical presence and human interaction, such as conducting services, providing spiritual guidance, and organizing community events. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like scheduling and communication, the core value of the role is in-person engagement and human connection, limiting cognitive AI exposure.

title: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Teacher assistants usually require completion of a 10-month college program in teaching assistance, educational assistance or other social science program.
Teacher assistants who help students with special needs usually require specialized training and experience.
exposure_rationale: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants perform a mix of interpersonal and administrative tasks. While AI can assist with some administrative duties like scheduling and record-keeping, the core of their role involves direct interaction with students, supporting teachers, and adapting to dynamic classroom environments. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in administrative efficiency, but human presence and judgment remain central to the role.

title: Other instructors
education_req: Enrollment in or completion of post-secondary school in a related discipline may be required for tutors, secondary and elementary school subjects.
Expertise in the field of instruction must be demonstrated.
Driving instructors must obtain special licensing as a driving instructor in the province of employment.
exposure_rationale: The role of 'Other instructors' involves a blend of teaching, coordination, and potentially some administrative tasks. While AI can assist with lesson planning, grading, and administrative coordination, the core teaching and interpersonal interaction require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools may handle some scheduling and communication tasks, but the primary instructional role remains less exposed to full automation.

title: Sheriffs and bailiffs
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or bachelor's degree in legal studies is usually required.
Sheriffs require experience as a deputy sheriff or bailiff and/or in custodial/escort or security work.
exposure_rationale: Sheriffs and bailiffs primarily perform duties that are physical and require a human presence, such as serving legal documents, enforcing court orders, and maintaining security in courtrooms. While AI could assist with scheduling and administrative tasks, the core responsibilities involve physical presence and human judgment, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools have minimal impact on the primary duties of this occupation.

title: Correctional service officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Post-secondary education in correctional services, police studies or criminology is required in New Brunswick, Quebec, and Alberta and Prince Edward Island.
Correctional officer recruits must successfully complete the Correctional Service of Canada training course to be employed by federal institutions.
exposure_rationale: Correctional service officers primarily engage in physical and interpersonal tasks such as supervising inmates, maintaining security, and responding to emergencies, which are not easily automated by AI. While AI may assist with administrative tasks like report writing or scheduling, the core responsibilities require human judgment and presence, limiting cognitive AI exposure.

title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field or experience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.

Zoning inspectors or zoning enforcement officers may require a college diploma as a civil engineering or architectural technologist or technician and several years of experience in enforcement and/or building construction or a related field.
exposure_rationale: By-law enforcement and regulatory officers perform tasks that involve a mix of fieldwork, interpersonal interactions, and administrative duties. While AI can assist with data analysis, report generation, and some routine communication tasks, the core responsibilities require human judgment, negotiation, and enforcement in varied situations. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in documentation and scheduling, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and physical presence required for enforcement activities.

title: Border services, customs, and immigration officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A four-week online training and a four-and-half-month specialized training at the CBSA College are required for border services officers.

Successful pass of the officer trainee entrance exam (OTEE), successful completion of the Canadian Firearms Safety Course (CGSC) and the Canadian Restricted Firearms Safety Course (CRFSC) is required for border services officers.
exposure_rationale: Border services, customs, and immigration officers perform a mix of physical presence tasks and administrative duties. While AI can assist with document processing, data analysis, and routine decision-making, human judgment and interpersonal skills are crucial for assessing situations and interacting with travelers. Tier 2 AI tools can streamline some administrative tasks, but Tier 3 automation is limited by the need for human oversight and decision-making in complex scenarios.

title: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces
exposure_rationale: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces primarily engage in physical and strategic tasks that require human judgment, adaptability, and presence. While AI may assist in logistical coordination and communication, the core duties involve hands-on and unpredictable field work, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools have minimal impact on the primary responsibilities of this occupation.

title: Home child care providers
education_req: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Home child care providers, parent's helpers and foster parents may require completion of a training program in child care or a related field.
Child care or household management experience may be required.
exposure_rationale: Home child care providers primarily engage in physical and interpersonal tasks such as supervising and caring for children, which are not easily automated by AI. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling or record-keeping, the core value of the job remains in the hands-on care and interaction with children, limiting cognitive AI exposure.

title: Home support workers, caregivers and related occupations
education_req: Some secondary school education is usually required.
Home management experience may be required.
College or other courses in home support may be required.
exposure_rationale: Home support workers and caregivers primarily engage in physical and interpersonal tasks such as assisting with daily living activities, providing companionship, and performing household duties. While AI may assist with scheduling and minor administrative tasks, the core value of this occupation is in-person care and support, which limits cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have minimal impact on the primary responsibilities of these roles.

title: Primary combat members of the Canadian Armed Forces
exposure_rationale: Primary combat members of the Canadian Armed Forces engage in highly physical and unpredictable tasks that require human presence and decision-making in dynamic environments. While AI may assist in logistics or strategic planning, the core combat duties are not significantly exposed to cognitive AI automation. The role's primary value is in physical execution and situational judgment, which are not easily replaced by AI systems.

title: Student monitors, crossing guards and related occupations
education_req: Completion of secondary school may be required.
First aid certification and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) training may be required.
exposure_rationale: The primary tasks of student monitors and crossing guards involve physical presence, supervision, and ensuring safety, which are not easily digitized or automated by AI. While AI can assist with scheduling or reporting, the core responsibilities require human judgment and interaction in real-world environments, limiting AI exposure to minimal levels.

title: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers
education_req: Library managers
A graduate degree in library and information science and several years of experience as a librarian, including supervisory experience are required.
Archive managers
exposure_rationale: Library, archive, museum, and art gallery managers are exposed to AI through tasks like cataloging, scheduling, and managing digital collections, which can be enhanced by LLM+tools and multi-agent systems. While human judgment and cultural expertise remain important, AI can significantly streamline administrative and coordination tasks, increasing exposure relative to base-LLM capabilities alone.

title: Managers - publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in communications, broadcasting, journalism or other arts discipline is usually required.
Several years of experience within the publishing, film production, broadcasting or theatre industry are usually required.
exposure_rationale: Managers in publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting, and performing arts are involved in coordination, communication, and decision-making tasks that are increasingly supported by AI tools.
