Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can assist with scheduling, project management, and data analysis, while Tier 3 systems may automate routine coordination tasks. However, human judgment and creative decision-making remain central, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors
education_req: A university degree in recreology, physical education, sports administration or a related field or a college diploma in recreation management or sports administration is usually required.
Several years of experience in an occupation related to recreation and sports administration, consulting or programming are usually required.
Some directors of recreation may be required to have a Municipal Recreation Director's Certificate.
exposure_rationale: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors engage in a mix of coordination, planning, and interpersonal tasks. AI tools can assist with scheduling, communication, and administrative duties, but human presence and judgment are essential for program development and client interaction. While Tier 2 AI can enhance productivity, the role's reliance on human creativity and leadership limits full automation potential.

title: Librarians
education_req: A master's degree in library science or a master's degree in library and information studies or a master's degree from a program accredited by the American Library Association is required.
exposure_rationale: Librarians are exposed to AI through tasks such as cataloguing, information retrieval, and managing digital resources, which can be enhanced by LLMs and LLM+tools. While human judgment and interpersonal skills remain important for community engagement and specialized research assistance, AI can automate routine information processing and database management tasks, increasing exposure to cognitive AI advancements.

title: Conservators and curators
education_req: Conservators require a master's degree in art conservation or completion of a two-year college program in conservation technology and several years of experience in conservation work.
Conservators may be accredited by the Canadian Association of Professional Conservators.
Curators require a master's or bachelor's degree in museology, art history or a field related to their specific area of work.
exposure_rationale: Conservators and curators engage in tasks that involve both physical handling of artifacts and digital coordination, such as cataloging, research, and exhibit planning. While AI can assist with research, data management, and some aspects of digital curation, the physical and judgment-intensive nature of conservation work limits full automation. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in administrative tasks, but Tier 3 systems are unlikely to replace the nuanced decision-making and hands-on conservation work required in this field.

title: Archivists
education_req: A bachelor's degree with a minor in archival studies or a university degree with a certificate in archival studies is required.
A master's degree in archival studies, archival studies and information science, library science or history may be required.
exposure_rationale: Archivists primarily engage in organizing, preserving, and managing collections of historical documents and records, which involves significant knowledge work. AI tools can assist with digitizing records, metadata tagging, and database management, enhancing productivity. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems could automate routine cataloging and retrieval tasks, but human judgment is still crucial for preservation decisions and historical context interpretation.

title: Editors
education_req: A bachelor's degree in English, French, journalism or a related discipline is usually required.
Several years of experience in journalism, writing, publishing or a related field are usually required.
Membership in the Editors Association of Canada may be required.
exposure_rationale: Editors are highly exposed to AI as their core tasks involve writing, reviewing, and editing text, which are areas where LLMs excel. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems can assist with grammar checking, content suggestions, and even automate parts of the editing process, significantly impacting productivity and workflow. While human judgment and creativity remain important, the digital nature of the work makes it highly susceptible to AI advancements.

title: Authors and writers (except technical)
education_req: Copywriters usually require a university degree or college diploma in French, English, marketing, advertising or another discipline.
Creative writing programs are offered by universities and colleges.
Talent and ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are important hiring criteria.
exposure_rationale: Authors and writers are highly exposed to AI, particularly through base LLMs that excel at writing, summarizing, and drafting. While creativity and unique voice remain human strengths, AI tools can assist with generating content, editing, and even mimicking writing styles. Tier 2 and Tier 3 systems can further automate tasks like research and content organization, increasing exposure significantly in this predominantly digital occupation.

title: Technical writers
education_req: A university degree in the area of specialization, such as computer science or engineering is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Technical writers are highly exposed to AI as their work involves writing, summarizing, and analyzing information, which are core capabilities of LLMs. Tier 2 tools can assist with research and drafting, while Tier 3 systems could automate entire documentation workflows, including updates and revisions. Human oversight remains important for accuracy and context, but the core tasks are squarely in the domain where AI is rapidly improving.

title: Journalists
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in journalism or a related field such as communications is usually required.
exposure_rationale: Journalists are highly exposed to AI as their work involves writing, summarizing, and analyzing information, which are core capabilities of LLMs. Tier 2 tools can assist in research, fact-checking, and drafting articles, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine reporting tasks. However, human judgment and creativity remain important for investigative journalism and nuanced storytelling, moderating the exposure slightly.

title: Translators, terminologists and interpreters
exposure_rationale: Translators, terminologists, and interpreters are highly exposed to AI due to advancements in language models that can perform translation and interpretation tasks. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with terminology management and translation memory, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine translation workflows. However, human oversight remains important for nuanced and context-sensitive translations, keeping the exposure from reaching the maximum level.

title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
education_req: Producers, directors and film editors

A university degree or college diploma in the performing arts, broadcasting, journalism, business administration, theatre production or film studies and experience in a technical or production occupation in motion pictures, broadcasting or theatre are usually required.
Choreographers
exposure_rationale: Producers, directors, and choreographers engage in creative and coordination tasks that involve significant human judgment and artistic vision, which are less susceptible to AI automation. However, AI tools can assist in scheduling, budgeting, and project management tasks, which are part of their roles. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can enhance productivity in these areas, but the core creative and leadership functions remain human-centric, keeping the overall exposure moderate.

title: Conductors, composers and arrangers
exposure_rationale: Conductors, composers, and arrangers engage in creative tasks that involve significant human judgment and artistic interpretation, which are less susceptible to AI automation. However, AI tools can assist in music composition, arrangement, and analysis, potentially enhancing productivity. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in scheduling performances or managing digital music libraries, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to replace the core creative and interpretive aspects of the role.

title: Musicians and singers
education_req: Musical training from a university or college or through private instruction is usually required.
Members of orchestras and other professional classical musicians and singers usually require a university degree in music.
Musical talent and ability, as demonstrated during an audition, are important hiring criteria.
exposure_rationale: Musicians and singers primarily engage in creative and performance-based tasks that require human presence and artistic interpretation, which are not easily replicated by AI. While AI can assist with music composition and production, the core value of live performance and personal artistry remains largely unaffected by digital automation. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have limited impact on the primary tasks of this occupation, which are inherently physical and creative.

title: Library and public archive technicians
education_req: Library technicians usually require completion of a two- to three-year college program in library and information technology.
Public archive technicians require completion of a college diploma or certificate program in archive and document management technology.
exposure_rationale: Library and public archive technicians engage in tasks such as cataloguing, organizing, and retrieving information, which are increasingly supported by AI tools. Tier 2 AI systems can assist with database management, indexing, and information retrieval, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine cataloguing and archiving processes. However, human oversight and judgment remain important for managing collections and assisting patrons, moderating the overall exposure.

title: Film and video camera operators
education_req: Completion of a college or technical program in broadcasting, audio-visual technology or a related field and experience as an assistant camera operator are usually required.
Creative and technical ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work and experience, may be required.
exposure_rationale: Film and video camera operators primarily engage in hands-on, creative work that involves operating cameras and capturing footage, which is not easily replaced by AI. While AI can assist in post-production tasks like editing and color correction, the core task of filming requires physical presence and artistic judgment. Therefore, AI exposure is low, as the job's value is largely in manual skill and creative decision-making rather than digital coordination or routine information processing.

title: Graphic arts technicians
education_req: A college diploma in commercial or graphic arts, computer graphics or animated design is required.
Experience or training in multimedia design at a post-secondary, college or technical institution may be required.
Creative ability and artistic talent, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are required.
exposure_rationale: Graphic arts technicians are highly exposed to AI as their work involves digital design, layout, and editing tasks that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can automate many aspects of design, such as generating layouts, optimizing images, and even creating basic designs. Multi-agent systems could further streamline workflows by automating routine design tasks and coordination, increasing exposure to AI-driven changes in the occupation.

title: Broadcast technicians
exposure_rationale: Broadcast technicians perform tasks that involve setting up, operating, and maintaining equipment for radio and television broadcasts, which are partially exposed to AI. While AI can assist in monitoring and adjusting audio and video levels, human judgment and technical skills are still crucial for troubleshooting and live event management. Tier 2 AI tools can aid in routine monitoring and diagnostics, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the need for human oversight in dynamic broadcast environments.

title: Audio and video recording technicians
education_req: Completion of a college or other program in recording engineering, audiovisual technology or a related field or experience as a recording studio assistant is usually required.
Senior occupations in this unit group, such as recording and sound engineers, require experience.
exposure_rationale: Audio and video recording technicians engage in tasks that involve both technical knowledge and hands-on work with equipment. While AI can assist with editing, mixing, and optimizing audio and video files, the physical setup and operation of recording equipment still require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools can enhance productivity in post-production tasks, but Tier 3 automation is less applicable due to the need for on-site technical adjustments and creative decisions.

title: Announcers and other broadcasters
education_req: Completion of a college radio or television arts program is usually required.
Practical training, such as work at a college radio station, may be required.
Talent and ability, as demonstrated during an audition, are important hiring criteria.
exposure_rationale: Announcers and broadcasters are exposed to AI through tasks like script writing, content summarization, and audience engagement analysis, which can be enhanced by LLMs. Tier 2 AI tools can assist with scheduling, content distribution, and social media management, increasing efficiency. However, the need for human presence, voice, and personality in live broadcasting and audience interaction still requires human involvement, preventing full automation.

title: Other technical and coordinating occupations in motion pictures, broadcasting and the performing arts
education_req: For most occupations in this unit group, completion of a college or university program in broadcasting, theatre arts or a related field and several years of experience in a related support or assisting occupation in motion pictures, broadcasting or the performing arts are required.
Floor managers require a portfolio of work.
Make-up artists working in motion pictures, broadcasting and the performing arts may require a training program.
exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a mix of technical and coordinating tasks in the arts and entertainment industry. While AI can assist with scheduling, communication, and some technical tasks, the need for human presence and judgment in creative and technical coordination remains significant. Tier 2 AI tools may enhance productivity, but Tier 3 automation is less likely to fully replace the nuanced and context-specific tasks in this field.

title: Graphic designers and illustrators
education_req: A university degree in visual arts with specialization in graphic design, commercial art, graphic communications or cartooning or completion of a college diploma program in graphic arts is required.
Experience or training in multimedia design at a post-secondary, college or technical institution may be required.
In addition to the arts, training in biology, engineering, architecture or a scientific field is usually required for medical, technical and scientific illustrators.

Telephone and switch network installers and repairers require completion of an apprenticeship program ranging from three to four years or a combination of over three years work experience in the trade and some high school, college or industry related courses.

Cable television service technicians require completion of a college program in electronics or a combination of college, correspondence or industry courses related to electronics and electrical systems and on-the-job training or completion of a four-year cable television technician apprenticeship program.
exposure_rationale: Telecommunications equipment installation and cable television service technicians primarily perform hands-on technical work involving installation, maintenance, and repair of equipment, which is not highly exposed to cognitive AI. While AI may assist in diagnostics or scheduling, the core tasks require physical presence and manual skills. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools have limited impact on the primary job functions, keeping the exposure low.

title: Plumbers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.

Trade certification is compulsory in Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Saskatchewan and Alberta and available, but voluntary, in Newfoundland and Labrador, Manitoba, British Columbia, the Yukon, the Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
exposure_rationale: Plumbers primarily engage in hands-on, physical work that involves installing, repairing, and maintaining plumbing systems. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks such as scheduling or inventory management, the core value of the occupation is manual skill and physical presence, which are not significantly impacted by cognitive AI tools. Therefore, the AI exposure remains low.
